P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions
Monday, 27 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06261 - 1 Lead Financial Analyst
Job details
The Technology Finance Practitioner supports the effective use and control of ICT financial resources to enable business priorities, compliance and risk mitigation. The role helps plan and manage Branch budgets and forecasts (including Opex and Capex), strengthens financial governance and reporting, and supports intake of new technology initiatives. It also provides clear financial advice to the ICT Branch Head and Directors to manage pressures and improve services to clients and corporate stakeholders.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Develop financial frameworks to improve budget and forecasts, particularly around assessing ICT Opex vs Capex.
• Develop and design ICT financial processes and governance for the Branch’s budgets.
• Assist in the development of ICT ‘front door’ for incoming technology initiatives.
• Develop mitigation strategies to address the existing financial pressures in Digital services.
• Provide strategic and operational financial advice to the ICT Branch Head.
• Assist the Branch’s Directors with budget carve information and link financial activities to their ICT business plans and priorities.
• Support the Branch’s Directors with building and maintaining ICT forecasts.
• Identify and develop opportunities to improve services to clients and corporate stakeholders.
• Provide ICT financial information and advice to support the preparation of statutory reporting obligations.
• Involvement in the New Policy Proposal process and ICT investment submissions.
• Provide ICT financial performance information against agreed metrics with supporting value-add commentary.
• Any other tasks, as directed by the Director.

Monday, 27 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06269 - 1 Program Manager
Job details
Program Managers coordinate the delivery of multiple related projects and other change activities, which may include business-as-usual activities.
Provision of Solution Manager-style strategic procurement and commercial advisory services on a time and materials basis. The role partners with Digital services to define outcomes, shape delivery and sourcing approaches, strengthen commercial capability, and advise senior decision-makers to achieve value-for-money results and compliance with the Commonwealth Procurement Rules.
Provision of specialist leadership across the end-to-end procurement and commercial lifecycle for digital initiatives—bringing a solution focus to investment choices, risk and governance, market engagement, and continuous improvement for complex, high-value/high-risk engagements.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Serve as a trusted strategic advisor to Digital services, providing commercially grounded guidance that supports solution delivery and ensures compliance with the Commonwealth Procurement Rules and alignment with departmental objectives.
• Lead discovery with business areas to clarify problems, outcomes and constraints, and translate these into fit-for-purpose sourcing and commercial strategies that optimise value for money.
• Provide strategic risk, issue and impact advice for complex procurements, including options analysis and clear recommendations to support executive decisions and governance forums.
• Build and sustain influential relationships with internal stakeholders, delivery teams and suppliers, including engaging confidently with senior executives to align on outcomes and trade-offs.
• Act as an escalation point, facilitating resolution of commercial and delivery issues and negotiating practical, mutually beneficial outcomes that protect departmental interests.
• Develop guidance, playbooks, templates and decision artefacts that standardise good practice and uplift solution, procurement and commercial capability across Digital services.
• Identify strategic opportunities to improve the digital sourcing pipeline, operating model and commercial outcomes, and advise on prioritisation and implementation pathways.
• Drive continuous improvement by sharing better practice approaches (including market engagement, contracting strategies and performance management) and embedding lessons learned.
• Prepare high-quality briefs, minutes and decision records that synthesise complex information into strategic analysis, options and recommendations.
• Translate complex commercial and procurement concepts into clear, actionable advice for technical and non-technical audiences.
• Design and support risk mitigation and assurance strategies across the procurement lifecycle (planning, sourcing, contracting and delivery) to safeguard departmental outcomes.
• Undertake other duties as directed to support Digital services and departmental strategic priorities.

Details: LH-06257 - Minimum of 2 Senior Infrastructure Engineer
Infrastructure Engineers implement, manage and retire the infrastructure (including physical, virtual, networking and cloud infrastructure) that supports digital services.
The Senior Infrastructure Engineer - SAP security uses their in-depth knowledge of specific ICT security models to provide expert advice on the creation and operational maintenance of system roles, access authorisations, and security profiles.
The Senior Infrastructure Engineer - SAP security works under the broad direction of senior staff to deliver quality outcomes. They exercise a considerable degree of independence, with decision-making substantially dependent on their high-level judgement and consideration of wider departmental implications. In performing a technical leadership role, the Senior Infrastructure Engineer - SAP security drives innovation, changes in workplace practices, and organises work in the context of competing priorities and strategic direction.
There is no on call work however, some tasks may need to be undertaken after business hours.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Creation and maintenance of SAP security roles for services Australia and our shared services agencies including NDIA and DVA
• Monitoring and development of SAP IDM
• Monitoring of development of SAP GRC
• Monitoring and development of SAP HANA security role
• Technical knowledge for any production SAP security issues
Essential criteria
1. Minimum 10+ years’ experience working as an expert SAP security Engineer/Analyst.
2. Expert technical knowledge of SAP security platforms (SAP ABAP, SAP IDM, SAP HANA, SAP GRC) and their implementation within large government agencies.
Desirable criteria
1. Sound technical knowledge of SAP security platforms (SAP ABAP, SAP IDM, SAP HANA, SAP GRC)
2. Knowledge and experience with SAP ABAP Profile Generator
3. SAP HANA role build experience
4. SAP GRC development, monitoring and role build experience
SAP IDM development and monitoring experience

P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Friday, 24 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06251 - 1 Senior SharePoint Specialist
Infrastructure Engineers implement, manage and retire the infrastructure (including physical, virtual, networking and cloud infrastructure) that supports digital services.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Provide senior specialist leadership across SharePoint Online (SPO) and Microsoft Purview to improve information governance, compliance outcomes, and platform sustainability, with a primary focus on storage consumption and cost control.
• Analyse and diagnose SPO storage consumption across sites, libraries, file versions, recycle bin/holds, content types, and governance settings to identify the main growth drivers, risks, and cost pressures.
• Assess and remediate legacy and existing SPO environments to identify and address:
inefficient information architecture and site sprawl
duplication and low‑value content accumulation
over‑retention or misaligned retention configuration
sensitivity/retention interactions that unintentionally increase storage
• Design and document a fit‑for‑purpose storage and information governance approach that integrates:
site and content lifecycle management
versioning and file history controls
retention, disposal, and archival patterns
alignment to Microsoft Purview Information Protection and Data Lifecycle/Records management capabilities
• Provide expert advice and hands‑on guidance on practical implementation of Microsoft Purview controls (e.g., retention labels/policies, sensitivity labels, records/record status, holds) and their downstream impact on SPO usability, compliance, and storage growth.
• Develop and prioritise pragmatic optimisation actions and roadmaps (e.g., version limits, site consolidation, clean‑up campaigns, information architecture improvements, archive patterns, policy refinement) balancing cost, compliance, risk, and user impact.
• Partner with platform owners and business stakeholders to define governance controls, clarify trade‑offs, and influence sustainable behaviours (e.g., storage‑aware working practices, lifecycle expectations, and content ownership).
• Produce concise, implementation‑ready documentation including technical guidance, configuration standards, governance procedures, and operational runbooks to support adoption and ongoing management.
• Support decision‑making through evidence‑based analysis and reporting, including cost estimates, risk/compliance implications, implementation effort, and measurable outcomes.
• Undertake other duties as directed.
Essential criteria
1. Expert level SharePoint Online experience, including strong capability across site administration, information architecture, permissions, governance, versioning and library/list configuration.
2. Demonstrated experience optimising SPO at scale, with a track record of identifying and reducing storage growth drivers and finding other storage savings.
3. Demonstrated experience influencing senior stakeholders on cost, risk, compliance, and optimisation decisions.
4. Demonstrated experience designing and implementing governance models for SPO, particularly those that support lifecycle management, storage disposal processes, sustainable operational practices, and role-based access.
Desirable criteria
1. Strong, practical Microsoft Purview experience, including retention policies, retention labels, sensitivity labels, information protection behaviours, and records management concepts.
2. Sound knowledge of information/records management principles in federal government and their practical application in Microsoft 365.
3. Experience with the development and implementation of PowerPlatform governance models and their application.

Details: LH-06178 - 1 Manager Data platform Admin Data Manager
Data Managers support organisations with the sourcing, acquisition, management, integration and sharing of data to provide business insights and drive data-based decision making.
Key duties and responsibilities
The successful Records Support Officer will be responsible for performing a range of records and information management functions, including:
• Accurately classify, capture, and store records in line with regulatory requirements and standards issued by the National Archives of Australia;
• Provide Content Manager system support to staff, including day‑to‑day user assistance, troubleshooting, and issue resolution;
• Deliver records and information management training and support to staff across the organisation;
• Carry out routine administrative tasks within Content Manager, such as mapping activities, user registrations, and permission checks;
• Coordinate the retrieval of physical records from offsite storage providers and undertake physical records management activities, including listing, filing, reporting, and tracking;
• Administer and maintain the Policy Library on the IBA intranet, ensuring documents remain current, controlled, and easily accessible;
• Provide operational support to the IAM project team during the transition from Content Manager to SharePoint, including information migration activities, user support, and change‑related tasks.
• Respond to ad hoc information management requests
Essential criteria
1. The candidate must demonstrate proven experience 60%
• Minimum 2 years supporting records or information management functions within a government or regulated environment
• Demonstrated knowledge and proven experience in the use of Content Manager/TRIM and MS teams/SharePoint.
• Experience in physical record management activities including liaising with offsite storage providers.
• Strong working knowledge of M365 especially SharePoint, MS teams, Outlook and Word.
• Ability to provide front line support for Content Manager/TRIM and MS teams, including:
o Responding to user enquiries
o Trouble shooting issues and escalating where appropriate
• Ability to train and support IBA staff in Content Manager and MS teams/SharePoint
2. The supplier must demonstrate a strong and genuine commitment to supporting Indigenous economic participation and development. This includes evidence of:
Indigenous ownership, leadership, or partnership arrangements, or active engagement with Indigenous‑owned businesses.
Employment, training, or professional development opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, including initiatives that build long‑term capability and career pathways.
Subcontracting or procurement practices that preference Indigenous suppliers, contributing to broader Indigenous business growth and sustainability.
Cultural competency and cultural safety, including demonstrated experience working respectfully and effectively with Indigenous organisations such as IBA.
Initiatives that support Indigenous economic development, such as mentoring, capability‑building programs, community engagement, or participation in Indigenous business networks.
3. • Working knowledge and experience with NAA standards and legislation
• Strong written and verbal communication skills and demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with a diverse range of stakeholders and teams.
• Strong focus in customer service, supporting staff in a responsive and professional manner
• Excellent time management skills and the ability to work unsupervised, handling competing priorities and fluctuating workloads.
• Ability to exercise discretion and maintain a high level of confidentiality.
• Demonstrated excellence in accuracy and attention to detail in a fast-paced environment meeting strict deadlines.

Details: LH-06262 - 1 Junior Tester
SFIA Level: 2
A SFIA 2 IT Test Analyst tests the function and interfaces of the systems which make up the Department’s core business processing systems, sourcing and analysing design documents, and assists in the preparation, peer-review and execution of test scripts.
A SFIA 2 IT Test Analyst works in an agile/iterative environment and utilises test management tools to prepare and execute test scripts, document results, analyse data, prepare test artefacts and escalate issues, defects and incidents in line with assigned work scope from the Scrum Master or Team Lead. They may be expected to lead sections of work within a release and be required to undertake specialist or technical research and analysis activities.
They communicate across a range of media types, using various styles to suit different audiences and understand and embrace the agency’s various ways of working and are agile in their approach to collaboration and delivery of services.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Plan, design, execute and review testing activities in line with departmental test strategies, standards and quality frameworks.
• Develop and maintain test plans, test cases, scripts, test data, environments and supporting documentation across waterfall and Agile projects.
• Manage defects through investigation, documentation, escalation and resolution in accordance with defect management processes.
• Collaborate with business, technical and stakeholder groups to clarify requirements, validate system behaviour and coordinate UAT.
• Provide testing estimates, progress reporting, metrics and quality assessments to support project planning and delivery.
• Support production releases through test completion, sign‑off and assurance of test deliverables.
• Contribute to continuous improvement of testing practices, tools and processes, and assist enterprise testing and QA activities.
• Provide guidance, mentoring and knowledge transfer, ensuring documentation is stored in accordance with recordkeeping requirements.
• Work with various testing methodologies including waterfall and Agile
• Adhere to the Defect management strategy to manage defects, gathering information for defect entries and identifying points of failure and organising resources in the investigation of issues
• Contribute to and review testing artefacts, test specifications
• Assist the Enterprise Testing and Quality Assurance section and other teams with testing activities.
Required skills and experience:
SFIA Level 2: Demonstrated experience (3+ years) in manual testing, including:
Desktop/Application-Seibel, .Net, (including D365), interagency, system integration, mobile device & browser stack testing.
Analysing documented specifications, user stories and requirements to support the creation of test cases
Write, review, and execute test cases.
Demonstrated ability to work under limited direction with competing priorities and be accountable for completion of work; and Demonstrated ability to adapt and work in a constantly changing environment.
Report and track the resolution of defects/issues as they are found in testing.
Work with the ability to be proactive, flexible, and agile, whilst managing changing goals and scope in a multidisciplinary team.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, and skilled at prioritisation and multi-tasking.
Ideally exposure to or previous experience in .Net, Seibel, Sales force applications
accessibility testing experience is desirable

Development finance entity package for renewable energy zone

Tenders.Net #: 1603060
Agency: Energy Corporation of NSW
Location: NSW
Closing: 11/05/2026 - 03:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Kathryn Coutts
E: nerez.dfe@energyco.nsw.gov.au

Additional details/instructions
To receive the Call for Registrations of Interest, an entity must complete and duly execute a Confidentiality and Disclaimer Deed Poll and Individual Deed Poll available with this BuyNSW notice, and submit by email to EnergyCo: nerez.dfe@energyco.nsw.gov.au.

Following receipt of completed and fully executed Confidentiality and Disclaimer Deed Poll(s) and Individual Deed Poll(s) from a bona fide entity, EnergyCo will provide access to the Call for Registrations of Interest to the entity via the Development Finance Entity Data Room.

To submit a registration, an interested party must return all returnable schedules available in the Call for Registrations of Interest by email to nerez.dfe@energyco.nsw.gov.au before the close date.
Details: ROI-2014632 - New England Renewable Energy Zone - Development Finance Entity Package - Call for Registrations of Interest
Call for Registrations of Interest for the Development Finance Entity Package for the New England Renewable Energy Zone.
Project goals
EnergyCo is leading the planning and coordination of Renewable Energy Zones (REZs) across NSW to meet the NSW Government's vision set out in the Electricity Infrastructure Roadmap.
The New England REZ is a critical energy project for NSW that will provide clean and reliable electricity to consumers for generations to come. The New England REZ network infrastructure project is the backbone of the New England REZ, and includes new transmission infrastructure, energy hubs and enabling infrastructure. The project is planned to be delivered in two stages to provide a transfer capacity of 6GW, with stage 1 to deliver 2.4GW by 2032 and stage 2 to deliver an additional 3.6GW by 2034.
To lower costs for NSW electricity consumers, EnergyCo will procure a ‘Development Finance Entity’ to provide lower-risk financing for land, biodiversity, contingency and other development costs, which are typically funded by Government on major infrastructure projects.
Additional information can be obtained from the EnergyCo website at: https://www.energyco.nsw.gov.au/ne.
Scope
The Development Finance Entity will be responsible for procuring and maintaining lower-risk financing for land, biodiversity, contingency and other development costs.
The Development Finance Entity will not be exposed to construction and operational risks, providing an opportunity to efficiently raise finance. This will also boost investment certainty, lower overall project costs, provide opportunities for alternate financing and lower costs for NSW electricity consumers. The successful entity (or consortium) will be appointed under a regulated concession model.
Recognising the long-term nature of the New England REZ, the Development Finance Entity Package presents a significant opportunity for an entity or a consortium to invest in a whole-of-life approach to the financing of the development and other related costs associated with New England REZ.
EnergyCo is seeking registrations from interested parties with bona fide capability and experience to deliver the Development Finance Entity Package, either as a single entity, as a fully formed consortium or through the demonstration of the ability to procure a fully formed consortium. In their registration, interested parties must provide evidence that entities have a proven track record and appropriate capability and experience financing projects of similar scale.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Details: 26.00001293840216 - B11009 (Tugun Tunnel) - St Helena Air conditioning Upgrade (Main Switch Board & Uninterrupted Power Supply room)
Description
Tenderers are invited for the B11009 (Tugun Tunnel) - St Helena Air conditioning Upgrade (Main Switch Board & Uninterrupted Power Supply room).
As outlined in the RFT suite of documentation.
---
This contract is for Upgradation and replacement of Computer Room Air conditioning (CRAC) units in St Helena & Tugun Bypass Tunnels server rooms.
This work consists of but not limited to the upgrading and replacing the existing CRAC Units and locating/potholing utilities on site.
All tenderers must comply with the Transport Statement of Business Ethics through the procurement process and any subsequent contract period.
All tenderers must comply with the NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework, NSW Government Supplier Code of Conduct and New South Wales Industrial Relations Guidelines: Building and Construction Procurement.

Details: Construction of the Woodburn Pony and Tennis Clubhouse.
This tender has been called to engage a suitably qualified and experienced civil organisation to construct the new Pony and Tennis Clubhouse in Woodburn, as per the design plans, bill of quantities and Part 3 - Specification. The clubhouse is located at Woodburn Oval (30 Wagner Street, Woodburn).
The scope of works include but not limited to:
• Planning approvals (e.g. BCA Assessment etc)
• Project and construction documents including but not limited to:
o Construction and Environmental management Plan
o Work Health and Safety
o Traffic and Pedestrian management
• Site establishment
• Site preparation including installation of water, gas, electrical and sewer
• Construction of footings
• Construction of clubhouse as per detailed designs including doors, windows and security screens, roof and drainage, painting of eaves, external stairs
• Construction of accessible ramp to clubhouse
• fit out of clubhouse including
o Male and female bathroom and changerooms, including showers
o accessible bathroom
o Lighting
o Kitchen
o security system
o Painting of internal walls and ceilings
• Connection of services completed including water, sewer, gas, electricity, security and alarms
• Commissioning of hot water and gas services
• Construction of gardens and turf around clubhouse
• Provide Works as Executed documentation to Council on completion of the project

Design consultancy services for electrification of leisure centre (various)

Tenders.Net #: 1603004
Agency: Camden Council
Location: NSW
Closing: 20/05/2026 - 03:00
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: Diane Ross
P: 132263
E: quotes@camden.nsw.gov.au
Details: T016/2026 - Design Consultancy services for the Electrification of Mount Annan Leisure Centre
Camden Council invites tenders for the Design Consultancy services for the Electrification of Mount Annan Leisure Centre. A detailed description of the requirements can be found in Council’s Request for Tender document.
SCOPE OF WORKS
Activity 1: Upgrade of Pool Hall and Spa Gas fired Air Handling Units (AHU)
Activity 2: Electrification of Domestic Hot water Units
Activity 3: Behind the Meter Renewable Energy Generation
Activity 4: Electrification of Cooking Appliances
SCOPE OF services
The Consultant shall provide comprehensive, multidisciplinary design services required to deliver the electrification upgrade at Mount Annan Leisure Centre. The services shall include investigation, design development, documentation, coordination of sub-consultants, and support during construction.
The Consultant will be responsible for leading and coordinating all design disciplines and ensuring that all outputs are fully integrated and aligned with project objectives. This includes reviewing existing documentation and feasibility studies, validating design assumptions, and developing practical and cost-effective design solutions.
A key requirement of the scope is the integrated design of the facility’s thermal systems, including the coordinated operation of heat pump systems with pool water heating and pool hall air handling (HVAC) systems. The Consultant shall ensure that all heating, ventilation, and associated energy systems are designed as a unified and optimized thermal system to deliver stable environmental conditions, improved efficiency, and reliable operation within a live aquatic facility.
All designs must comply with relevant Australian Standards, the National Construction Code, and applicable authority requirements. The Consultant shall ensure that all deliverables are suitable for tendering and construction purposes. Documentation shall be sufficient for a construct only Tender to be advertised and successfully delivered.
The Consultant is also responsible for identifying and mitigating all design, investigation, and integration risks associated with delivering the project within an existing operational facility and shall incorporate appropriate design responses to ensure a coordinated, compliant, and buildable outcome.
1 Review & Validation
• Review all existing documentation, including:
o Electrification Feasibility Study
o Type 2 Energy audit (AS/NZS 3598)
o Existing drawings, asset data, and reports
• Validate feasibility assumptions and confirm:
o System capacities
o Spatial requirements
o Integration constraints
• Identify gaps, risks, and opportunities
2 Site Investigations & Existing conditions
• Undertake detailed site inspections and investigations, including:
o Existing plantrooms and services
o electrical infrastructure (switchboards, loads, distribution)
o Mechanical systems (boilers, AHUs, ductwork)
o Hydraulic systems (hot water, pipework)
o Roof condition and structural capacity (for solar PV)
o Existing BMS system
• Confirm:
o Available space for new plant (internal/external)
o access, constructability, and staging constraints
• Coordinate and manage:
o Structural assessments
o Hazardous materials (if required)
o Service locating and utility investigations
3 Concept & Options Development
• Develop design options for:
o Heat pump systems (ASHP, CO₂ systems)
o Plantroom layouts (internal vs external)
o Domestic Hot water (DHW) system configurations
o Solar PV layouts
• Assess options against:
o Cost
o Energy performance
o Constructability
o Operational impacts
• Present recommended option to Council
• Recommended Option Report including justification
4 Detailed Design (All Disciplines)
Mechanical services
• Design of:
o Air Source Heat Pump systems (~1,100kW)
o Hydronic air handling systems
o Heat recovery systems
o Ventilation systems (100% outside air)
• Full integration of new heat pump systems with existing pool water heating and pool hall HVAC systems, ensuring coordinated thermal control, energy sharing where applicable, and stable temperature and humidity conditions within the aquatic environment
• Plant sizing, redundancy, staging strategy and acoustic performance review.
• Recommended maintenance schedules and required equipment clearance for ease of access for routine inspections and maintenance.
• Durability in varying environmental conditions
electrical services
• Assessment and upgrade (or augmentation) of the Main Switchboard (MSB)
• Load analysis and electrical capacity assessment
• New power distribution for:
o Heat pumps
o DHW systems
o Kitchen equipment
• Solar PV system design (~180kW)
• Integration with Council systems
Hydraulic services
• Design of:
o CO2 heat pump hot water systems
o Storage tanks and reticulation
• Pipework modifications and integration
• Drainage and overflow provisions
Structural Engineering
• Assessment of:
o Roof capacity for solar PV
o Plant loads and supports
• Design of:
o Plant platforms and supports
o Structural modifications (if required)
BMS & controls
• Design of upgraded Building management System (BMS)
• Integration of:
o Mechanical systems
o Energy monitoring systems
• Optimisation for:
o Energy efficiency
o Operational control
The primary objective of this upgrade is to integrate all systems into a single Building management System (BMS), enabling centralised control, enhanced automation, and compatibility across all systems, along with remote monitoring capabilities for efficient operation and performance management. Ability to integrate with existing renewable energy systems.
5 Coordination & Integration
• Lead full multidisciplinary coordination
• Ensure:
o All systems are integrated and functional
o No clashes between disciplines
• Coordinate with:
o Council stakeholders
o facility operator (e.g., BlueFit)
o Utility providers (e.g., Endeavour Energy)
6 Approvals & Compliance
7 Cost Planning & Value Engineering
8 Tender & Construction Documentation
9 Construction Phase services
10 Commissioning & Handover Support
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Occupancy opportunity

Tenders.Net #: 1602984
Agency: Bradfield Development Authority
Location: NSW
Closing: 14/07/2026 - 03:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: EOI Manager
E: bradfielddevelopment@deloitte.com.au

An Industry Briefing on this EOI will be held via ON24 at 10:00am on 13 May 2026. If you would like to attend, please RSVP via Enterprise Precinct RSVP. To register, please refer to the link provided.

Interested parties may submit an Expression of Interest by completing the Returnable Schedule at Part E and lodging an EOI Response electronically via the Lodgement Portal (reference: BDA001283).
Details: BDA001283 - Call for Occupant Expressions of Interest
This is a Call for Occupant Expressions of Interest (EOI). It invites organisations interested in locating within Bradfield City’s Enterprise Precinct, as either an owner occupier or tenant, to register their interest ahead of a future market process to appoint a master developer for the site.
Expressions of Interest are invited from businesses and other organisations interested in occupancy within the Enterprise Precinct that demonstrate:
• Commitment to contributing to the vision of an innovation-driven Enterprise Precinct.
• Industrial or operational activities that contribute to advanced technologies, research, collaboration or knowledge sharing in the Precinct. In particular, activities in advanced industries such as aerospace, clean technology, construction & advanced materials, defence, education & research, freight & logistics technologies, advanced manufacturing, semiconductor technology or circular economy, or other activities that support the Precinct’s growth and economic development goals.
• An interest or willingness to be part of a thriving sustainable Enterprise Precinct by collaborating with other entities that locate here and seek to leverage proximity to the NSW Government’s $300M investment in the Advanced Manufacturing Readiness facility (AMRF).
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Recyclables receival & processing services

Tenders.Net #: 1602953
Agency: Central Coast Council
Location: NSW
Closing: 02/06/2026 - 02:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Kellie Neville
Procurement Officer
P: 43047098
E: kellie.nevilIe@centralcoast.nsw.gov.au
Details: CPA/6743 - Recyclables Receival and Processing services
Central Coast Council invites submissions from suitably qualified and experienced contractors for the provision of Recyclables Receival and Processing services. This Request for Tender (RFT) forms part of Council’s broader program to modernise waste and resource recovery service delivery and to establish a new long-term processing arrangement to replace existing contractual arrangements.
The RFT seeks to appoint a contractor capable of delivering reliable, transparent and high-performing recyclables processing services that support Council’s environmental objectives, community expectations and regulatory obligations.
The proposed contract term is:
• Initial Term: 10 years (commencing Monday, 1 March 2027)
• Extension Options: 3 years + 2 years (at Council’s discretion)
• Maximum Term: 15 years.
Currently Council has an existing contract to process dry recyclables from the yellow bin services which ends January 2027.
Council has a statutory obligation to provide Domestic Waste Collection services under the Local Government Act 1993 and obligations to divert waste from landfill in accordance with the waste minimisation hierarchy under the Waste Avoidance and Resource Recovery Act 2001.
The ‘Yellow Bin’ recycling service is well established internationally and across Australia and is recognised as a core service as part of the Domestic Waste Collections provided to communities.

Details: PCC25/26-216 - Real Festival 2026_Party Hire
Council is seeking a suitably qualified and experienced supplier to provide a wide range of party hire infrastructure to support the safe and effective delivery of Real Festival over a two-year period, covering Real Festival 2026 and Real Festival 2027. This includes (but is not limited to) the provision of marquees, tables, chairs, fencing, equipment, and associated installation and pack-down services.
While the 2027 festival dates are not yet confirmed, it is anticipated that the event will follow similar scheduling and operational requirements to Real Festival 2026.

Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*

casey.mcgrath@tafensw.edu.au
Details: WS2098701993. Timber Teaching Consumables.
This RFP relates to the procurement of Timber and associated Teaching consumables to support the teaching of Construction, shopfitting, allied timber trades and associated courses.
TAFE NSW requires the supply and delivery of a broad range of timber and associated teaching consumables across NSW to nominated campus locations. These materials are required to support the teaching of Construction & carpentry and associated courses.
This procurement activity will have 2 categories to cover a broad range of Timber consumables, including but not limited to:
- Construction timbers and Commercial offer for items not specifically identified
Items may include, but will not be limited to, framing pine, pre primed moulding, V jointed pine lining boards and pre primed door jambs.
- General Hardware items and Commercial offer for items not specifically identified
Items may include, but will not be limited to, bullet head nails and Phillips head screws.
More than one supplier may be selected, and the contract term is expected to be for Three (3) years with two (2) optional 12-month extensions.

Details: RFT-2014641. NSW Police Academy Boiler Heaters Replacement.
Goulburn Academy's existing gas-fired boiler heating plant has reached end of life and requires replacement to maintain reliable building heating in Towers 1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 8 and Building D.
The Contractor is to Design & Construct the new Gas Boiler Heating System.
Scope:
Option 1 - Supply & Install New System
Full replacement of heating plant and ancillary equipment with modern, high-efficiency systems designed for 20+ years of reliable service.
Option 2 - Minimum Boiler Replacement
Replace the failed boilers while retaining and re-using the existing ancillary equipment.
All Contractors visiting and working in the Police Academy must obtain ESP Police Vetting Approval.

Audit, Risk & Improvement Committee - independent member

Tenders.Net #: 1602942
Agency: Uralla Shire Council
Location: NSW
Closing: 21/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: For further information, contact:
Steven Williams
P: (02) 6778 6300
E: council@uralla.nsw.gov.au

Complete the nomination form on the link provided and submit to:
E: council@uralla.nsw.gov.au
Details: EOI - Independent Member to ARIC
Uralla Shire Council is seeking Expressions of Interest (EOI) from people interested in joining the Uralla Shire (USC) and Walcha Councils' (WC) audit, Risk and Improvement Committee (ARIC) as an External Independent Member, with both councils seeking a common external member.
Both USC and WC seek to leverage shared experiences in similar matters of governance for issues that are often common across each Council, both in size and scope.
Each ARIC meeting will be scheduled for the same day with travel between each Council of approximately 45KM. ARIC agenda business paper formats are sent electronically to each member.
The Councils are inviting expressions of Interest from suitably qualified individuals for appointment as an external independent member to the USC and WC ARICs.
The audit, Risk and Improvement Committee is a key component of Councils’ governance frameworks. The Committees will be responsible for assisting Councils to fulfil their governance and oversight responsibilities.
The audit, Risk and Improvement Committee is comprised of three independent external members. Each Council has nominated a councillor to the committee (non-voting). Both Councils will be expecting the new member to commence duties at the audit, Risk and Improvement Committee meeting to be held 1 June 2026. An induction will be scheduled in May.
The Committee meets at least four times a year. Currently we have scheduled meetings for 1 June 2026, 7 September 2026 and 7 December 2026, The Committee is a formally appointed Committee of each Council. The appointment will be for a four-year term.
The current meeting fees for an independent member is $750 per meeting per Council.
In addition, Council will cover reasonable travel and accommodation costs.

Tenders.Net #: 1602898

Location: NSW
Closing: 12/05/2026 - 10:00 am
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown

Marisa Malherbe
P: 02 6861 2333
E: council@parkes.nsw.gov.au
Details: PSC2026/025 (2026025) - Peak Hill Pool Internal Refurbishment and Associated Works
Council is seeking submissions from suitably qualified contractors for the design, supply, and installation of an internal lining system or approved alternative surface treatment for the Peak Hill Swimming Pool. The works aim to address surface deterioration, restore watertight integrity, and extend the operational life of the asset.
The contract will be delivered under a Design and Construct (D&C) model, allowing tenderers to propose either a commercial-grade pool liner system or an alternative surface rehabilitation solution suitable for public pool use. The successful contractor will be responsible for investigation, design, installation, and commissioning of the proposed system.
The works are to be completed within the off-season period, with practical completion required prior to the October 2026 pool season.
Comprehensive details outlining the required scope of services are provided in Volume 3 – Specification of this Tender document.
Contract Completion Date: 2 October 2026.

Funding - Short-term property spaces (creative & cultural use)

Tenders.Net #: 1602850
Agency: City of Sydney
Location: NSW
Closing: 19/05/2026 - 04:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Grants team
P: 02 9265 9333
E: grants@cityofsydney.nsw.gov.au

Attend an open day or information session
Visit 107 Redfern Street to walk through the spaces on offer and hear how to apply.

Drop in on our open days to explore the space:
• Saturday 2 May, 11am to 6pm
• Friday 8 May, 11am to 6pm

Join an information session and guided walk-through of the site:
Thursday 23 April, 12pm to 1pm
• Wednesday 29 April, 1pm to 2pm
• Tuesday 5 May, 5:
• 30pm to 6:30pm
• Wednesday 13 May, 12pm to 1pm
Details: Bring Your Cultural and Creative Ideas to Life in Redfern
Apply for one of our short-term property spaces at 107 Redfern Street.
Use of 107 Redfern Street
At its meeting on 17 March 2025, Council resolved to prioritise local Aboriginal creative and cultural use of 107 Redfern Street.
Redfern is recognised as a significant place of Aboriginal life, resistance and self-determination, with an enduring history of community-led movements, cultural pride and leadership.
About this opportunity
We have 6 spaces available at 107 Redfern Street, Redfern for cultural and creative use through our short-term empty properties program.
This 2-storey industrial-style facility is in the heart of Redfern.
• Tenancy length: 12 months
• Cost: $100 a week per space plus GST.
• Consideration may be given to individual circumstances and rates may vary.
For detailed information about the spaces, read the applicant information pack.
Cultural and creative leaders, artists, knowledge holders and creative workers are invited to apply for a space, a combination of spaces, the full ground floor or first floor. All spaces will be unfurnished.
We strongly encourage Aboriginal-led and community-controlled organisations and cultural practitioners to apply.
This opportunity is designed to activate 107 Redfern Street while we consider its future as a centre for Aboriginal creativity and culture.
Eligible applicants can apply for a cash grant from the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander collaboration fund to support the activation, establishment or programming of the spaces.
About the short-term empty properties program
We own several commercial and community properties, including retail and office space. These properties may be empty for short periods between tenancies.
The short-term empty properties program provides immediate and temporary occupancy of under-used properties within our portfolio. The properties are offered for at least one month.
Our program supports:
• creative practitioners and cultural organisations
• community organisations and social enterprises
• businesses and startups that support the local economy
• organisations with a focus on sustainability and the environment.
Preference is given to applicants in our area or to projects that can demonstrate significant benefit to local communities.
Funding priorities
• Increasing opportunities for local Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander organisations
• Activities that reflect our cultural, social, economic and environmental strategies and action plans
• Boosting connection with the local community, including artists and creative organisations, small business and visitors
• Stimulating local economies by encouraging a diverse business mix with innovative or capacity building enterprises
• Furthering opportunities and exposure for emerging and established enterprises including artists.
Eligibility
You must show how your proposal benefits our residents, workers and visitors.
Applicants must demonstrate that payment of standard rates would cause financial hardship.
These individuals and organisations can apply:
• Eligible not-for-profit organisation
• Incorporated association
• Unincorporated community group auspiced by an eligible not-for-profit organisation
• Corporation
• Co-operative
• Partnership
• Sole trader
• Trustee of a trust.
This program is open to for-profit entities.
For the definition of each eligible applicant above. See the glossary/definitions section in the grants and sponsorship guidelines.
For more information about eligibility, see the grants and sponsorship guidelines.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Advertising billboards - operate, manage & maintain

Tenders.Net #: 1602660
Agency: Transport for NSW
Location: NSW
Closing: 08/07/2026 - 03:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 29/05/2026. Please confirm with contact.

David Hanna
E: david.hanna@transport.nsw.gov.au
P: 0448 301 602
Details: WS5622271244 - Existing advertising billboards
TfNSW is looking to partner with out-of-home (OOH) supplier/s to operate, manage and maintain 62 static and 34 digital OOH advertising roadside assets located in metro and regional NSW.
Project goals
Proposals from Respondents to undertake the development, management and operation of Existing Advertising assets (static and digital billboard assets) including the upgrade of static assets to digital and the decommissioning of static assets within this RFP.
The proposed contract terms are:
• Digital billboards – 5 years, plus a single, 2-year option
• Static billboards – 3 years, plus a single, 2-year option
• Static billboards upgraded to digital – 7 years, plus a single, 2-year option
Scope
The Supplier must work collaboratively with TfNSW to achieve the following key objectives:
• meet and exceed the outcomes, obligations and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in the Proposed Contract;
• efficiently plan and obtain DA consents from the Department of Planning and Environment (DPE) to upgrade static assets to digital;
• build state-of-the-art digital Advertising assets in a timely, efficient, and safe manner;
• position the in-scope Advertising assets as a premium asset within the OOH marketplace;
• provide a strong commercial revenue stream to TfNSW that exceeds annual sales targets and grows year on year;
• minimise exposure to various risks by adhering to relevant standards, policies and legislation (safety, development protocols, planning, advertising content standards etc)
• innovate to ensure the increasing sustainability of advertising revenue generated;
• report performance metrics to TfNSW; and
• propose new advertising strategies to TfNSW that are consistent with the requirements of TfNSW.

Details: RFT-2014405 - Acmena Clinical services Block
The purpose of the works is to provide a new clinical facility for Acmena Youth Justice Centre.
A separate request for tender, RFT-2013474 – Acmena Major Periodic maintenance (MPM) Works, is anticipated to be issued in late April and the construction of both projects will be delivered concurrently by NSW Public Works.
Scope
The design development and construction (DD&C) of a new Clinical services Block. The works include:
• Design development and documentation
• Site preparation and structural foundations
• Construction and fit out of the new building
• Supply and installation of fittings, furniture and equipment (FF&E)
• Related files.
Description
1 Design Development and Documentation
2 Principal Certifier (Crown Certificate)
3 Preliminaries
4 Tree Removal and Earthworks
5 Substructure
6 Wall and Roof Framing
7 Roofing
8 Brickwork and Cladding
9 Windows, Doors and Hardware
10 Wall, Floor and Ceiling Finishes
11 Fittings, Furniture and Equipment (FF&E)
12 Hydraulic services
13 electrical services
14 Mechanical services
15 security services
16 External Works
17 Testing, Commissioning, Training and Manuals
18 Building Certification, incl compliance with Review of Environmental Factors (REF)

Commercial advisory services for transmission project

Tenders.Net #: 1598857
Agency: Energy Corporation of NSW
Location: NSW
Closing: 04/05/2026 - 03:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 29/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

Nidhi Sharma
E: nidhi.a.sharma@energyco.nsw.gov.au

Final Date for queries on RFT: 3:00 pm (AEST) 28 April 2026
Details: SR01038 - Commercial Advisory services for Hunter Transmission Project
EnergyCo seeks one qualified provider with an experienced commercial team to manage commercial and regulatory functions for the Hunter Transmission Project during delivery.
Project goals
The NSW Government has developed the NSW Electricity Infrastructure Roadmap (the Roadmap) to transition NSW to a cleaner future and ensure this new supply of electricity can be reliably distributed to consumers.
To maintain the energy supply to the Hunter, Sydney and Illawarra regions, 500 kilovolt (kV) transmission connections are required between Eraring Power Station and Bayswater Power Station in Liddell in the Upper Hunter Valley.
The HTP is both a critical State significant infrastructure (CSSI) project and a Priority Network Infrastructure Project (PNIP). As the remaining coal-fired power stations close, the HTP is critical for energy security in New South Wales (NSW).
Scope
EnergyCo seeks to engage a single qualified Service Provider to deliver an experienced Commercial team that will support the Hunter Transmission Project during the delivery phase. The Commercial team will provide specialist commercial, regulatory, financial and contract management capability to ensure strong oversight of the project’s commercial and regulatory functions, including management of interfaces with the Network Operator and Third Parties.
The Service Provider will supply a team of six professional roles forming a core component of EnergyCo’s delivery‑phase Commercial function. These resources will maintain oversight of commercial, legal and regulatory activities, contribute to project governance, and help ensure effective delivery of key milestones. They will operate as part of an integrated project team, working closely with EnergyCo staff and stakeholders to support timely decision‑making, manage risks and maintain continuity across commercial activities.

Electric vehicle fleet charging infrastructure

Tenders.Net #: 1332542
Agency: NSW Procurement
Location: NSW
Closing: 11/04/2027 - 11:59 PM
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 11:59 pm & 12:00 pm on 01/12/2025. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity contact, details and link updated.

GORDON RISTEVSKI
E: NSWGFleetElectrification@treasury.nsw.gov.au

End date: 11-Apr-2027 11:59 PM (Previously advertised as 01-Dec-2025 & 11-Apr-2026)
End intake date: 11-Apr-2027 11:59 PM (Previously advertised as 5:00 pm on 01-Dec-2025 & 11-Apr-2026)
Details: SCM13381 - NSW Government Electric Vehicle Fleet Charging Infrastructure Prequalification Scheme
Description
Deliver the full scope of electric vehicle supply equipment goods, installation works and maintenance services.
Scope
The Electric Vehicle Fleet Charging Infrastructure Scheme is used by NSW Government Agencies and Non-Agency Eligible customers such as State owned corporations, councils and not-for-profit (“Buyers”). ​
​The approved supplier must provide quotations as requested by an eligible buyer to be considered for work, and must not charge over the maximum rates submitted under the scheme. The supplier may submit lower rates and prices than the rates and prices under the scheme rates card when tendering for a project with an eligible buyer.​
​The scope of work, fees and other requirements for a project agreement will be formed using the standard form agreement (see supporting documents). Approved suppliers under the scheme either directly, or via the engagement of its selected sub-contractors remain responsible for the delivery of the EVSE goods, works and services under the project agreement formed.​
​At the completion of each project the approved suppliers must submit a detailed project report to the Department.
---
The NSW Government committed in 2021 to transitioning the government fleet of passenger vehicles to electric by 2030 as part of the NSW Electric Vehicle Strategy. https://www.energy.nsw.gov.au/sites/default/files/2022-09/nsw-electric-vehicle-strategy-210225.pdf
Specifically, the Government set out goals to have 50% of newly purchased/leased passenger vehicles to be electric by 2026 and 100% of newly purchased/leased passenger vehicles to be electric by 2030.
Achieving these targets requires Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure to be set up at vehicle base locations such as NSW Government sites and depots.
The NSW Government Electric Vehicle Fleet Charging Infrastructure Prequalification Scheme SCM13381 (“Scheme”) is open to applications from companies seeking to be prequalified to deliver electrical Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE) Goods, Works and services under Capability A.
Capability A - electrical Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE) Goods, Works and services (as defined under the Scheme conditions).
Applicants must accept the Scheme conditions and satisfy all requirements as a condition of prequalification under the Scheme.
Prequalification under the Scheme is not a guarantee of work, continuity of the inclusion on the Scheme, receipt of opportunities to make submissions; or that engagements or work of any kind or quantity will be offered.
The Scheme will be open for an initial term of three (3) years.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Surface-based civil infrastructure works

Tenders.Net #: 1603070
Agency: Queensland Hydro
Location: QLD
Closing: 04/05/2026 - 02:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: indirectsprocurement@qldhydro.com.au
Details: RFT-BUR-1009A - Surface Civils Expression of Interest
Queensland Hydro is seeking Expressions of Interest from suitably qualified and experienced construction contractors to deliver surface-based civil infrastructure works in support of the Borumba Pumped Hydro Energy Storage (PHES) Project Exploratory Works.
The scope comprises enabling surface civil works required to provide safe access, working areas, and temporary water supply to support exploratory tunnelling, geotechnical drilling, and associated investigation activities across the project site. The successful respondent will be appointed as Principal Contractor and will be responsible for overall site management, including interface coordination within a multi-contractor and environmentally constrained operating environment.
The works are located in steep, undeveloped terrain and require a contractor with demonstrated capability in managing complex interfaces, traffic and access management, and engagement and coordination of specialist subcontractors. The Principal Contractor will also be responsible for sourcing, managing, and coordinating geotechnical drilling subcontractors in accordance with Queensland Hydro requirements.
The Exploratory Works are investigative in nature and are distinct from the Borumba PHES Main Works. They will provide critical geotechnical and site information to inform design development, future project procurement, and preparation of the Project Environmental Impact Statement (EIS).
This Expression of Interest forms the first stage of a two-stage procurement process:
Expression of Interest (EOI): April – May 2026
Request for Tender (RFT): June 2026 (shortlisted respondents only)
Anticipated Contract Award: September 2026.

Details: DPI26118 - Provision of Grounds maintenance services for DPI’s Toowoomba facility
The Department of Primary Industries (DPI), through the Agriculture business group, seeks the provision of grounds maintenance services for the DPI Toowoomba facility (203 Tor Street, Toowoomba) for a term of two (2) years. The Tor Street facility is 8.7-hectares providing office accommodation, storage, laboratory and trial crop facilities for several hundred Departmental and other state government agency staff.
This activity seeks to appoint one (1) supplier to complete the following ground maintenance tasks:
• Lawn Mowing
• Hedges, Shrubs & Edge Trimming
• Weed control
• Green Waste Removal
• Refuse Collection
• Mulch, Planting & Seeding
• Roads, Kerbs & Stormwater Pit Clearing
• Irrigation of Plants & Garden Beds
• Trimming of Established Trees
• Hardstand & Pathways Pressure Cleaning
The complete contract requirements are specified in Schedule 1 of the ‘Contract Details’.

Reinsurance broker services

Tenders.Net #: 1602909
Agency: Queensland Treasury
Location: QLD
Closing: 22/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: procurement@treasury.qld.gov.au
Details: QT20265683 - Provision of Reinsurance Broker services for the Queensland Government Insurance Fund
The Queensland Government Insurance Fund (QGIF) is seeking the services of an experienced reinsurance broker to advise on, structure, place and manage its reinsurance programs. These services support the State of Queensland’s risk transfer strategy by protecting against major catastrophic losses and significant liability exposures, and include natural hazard modelling, market advisory services, placement of reinsurance covers, and claims recoveries management. The objective is to secure cost-effective and appropriate reinsurance protection for the State’s insured assets and liabilities, ensuring financial resilience and stability across whole-of-Government.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Drone services

Tenders.Net #: 1602906
Agency: Port of Townsville
Location: QLD
Closing: 11/05/2026 - 10:00 am
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Procurement and Contracts
P: (07) 47811500
E: tenderlodgements@townsville-port.com.au
Details: RFT000028 - Provision of Drone services
The objective of the drone service is to provide a safe, efficient, and high-quality aerial capability to support operational, safety, environmental, and asset management activities across the Port of Townsville precinct. The service is intended to enhance situational awareness, improve the timeliness and accuracy of data collection, reduce reliance on manual or high-risk inspection methods, and support informed decision-making. Drone operations are expected to be conducted in compliance with all applicable CASA aviation, maritime, safety, security, and privacy requirements while minimising disruption to Port of Townsville operations.
The Port of Townsville operates within a complex, high-risk environment characterised by critical infrastructure, vessel movements, heavy machinery, and constrained access areas. Traditional inspection and monitoring methods can be resource intensive, pose safety risks, and may not provide the level of coverage or frequency required for effective port management. Drones now provide a reliable means of capturing high-resolution imagery and data over land and water, enabling more proactive maintenance, enhanced monitoring, and improved incident response. As a result, the Port of Townsville seeks to engage a qualified service provider to deliver professional drone services aligned with its operational and regulatory context.

Notice: Aerodrome operations services

Tenders.Net #: 1602666
Agency: City of Moreton Bay
Location: QLD
Closing: Not Stated
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Opportunity details updated.

E: procurement@moretonbay.qld.gov.au

All related communications will only be facilitated through Public Tenders forum within the Tender advertisement period.

To ensure regard to ethical behaviour and fair dealing, please refrain from approaching or discussing this opportunity directly with Councillors, Councillor nominees or Council employees.

Council will not accept any Tender submissions in response to this Advance Tender Notice.

Council does not warrant or represent that all information contained in this Advance Tender Notice is accurate or complete.

Council does not guarantee this Advance Tender Notice will result in a Request for Tender, and any changes remain at Council’s sole and absolute discretion.
Details: Advanced Tender Notice - Aerodrome Operations services - Redcliffe Aerodrome YRED
The City of Moreton Bay (Council) provides Prospective Tenderers with an Advanced Tender Notice on its intent to invite Tenders for Aerodrome Operations services – Redcliffe Aerodrome (YRED).
BACKGROUND
City of Moreton Bay (Council) is the owner and operator of the Redcliffe Aerodrome (YRED) located on Wirraway Drive, Rothwell. The Redcliffe Aerodrome is used by recreational and commercial users, which includes flight training schools, small chartered flights, aircraft manufacturing and maintenance business, as well as community groups.
Council is seeking the services of an experienced aerodrome management provider for the delivery of aerodrome operations services through the provision of a suitably qualified Aerodrome Reporting Officer (ARO), operational management, facility management, ensure compliance to relevant safety standards, quality assurance protocols and ensuring industry best practice is met.
Council views this collaboration as essential to achieving operational efficiency and advancing Council’s broader objectives.
TENDER DETAILS
Council anticipates the opportunity to Tender will be invited via a Request for Tender through a separate event in May 2026.

Design and construction of new dwellings

Tenders.Net #: 1599427
Agency: Yarrabah Aboriginal Shire Council
Location: QLD
Closing: 07/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown

E: procurement@yarrabah.qld.gov.au

Site Briefing: 15/04/2026 10AM
Details: YASC-2026-004 - RFT - Ambrym Street Design and Construction of 10 X New Dwellings
The Ambrym Street Housing Project comprises the design and construction of ten (10) new residential dwellings within a proposed subdivision at Ambrym Street. The development will deliver a mix of dwelling types in accordance with the issued architectural drawings and site plan.
The Contractor is responsible for the full design development, coordination and construction of the Works, including all associated civil works, services infrastructure and external works required to deliver a complete and operational housing development.
The Works shall be delivered in accordance with Remote Housing Standards and all applicable codes, standards and authority requirements, and shall result in dwellings that are fit for purpose and ready for occupation.
The Scope is described in the document(s) attached to or provided with this Request for Tender and identified as:
Scope of Works (Design & Construct)
The Contractor shall undertake the complete design, coordination, certification and construction of ten (10) new residential dwellings at Ambrym Street Stage 1 as follows;
• House Type 1 x four (4)
• House Type 2 x three (3)
• House Type 3 x one (1)
• House Type 4 x two (2)
The Works include, but are not limited to, all labour, materials, plant, equipment, design, approvals and activities necessary to deliver a fully operational, compliant and complete housing development fit for purpose and ready for handover and occupation.
Reference Documents
• Architectural Drawings:
o 2601 Yarrie Homes – House Type 1 (2 Bed Duplex)
o 2601 Yarrie Homes – House Type 2 (3 Bed)
o 2601 Yarrie Homes – House Type 3 (4 Bed)
o 2601 Yarrie Homes – House Type 4 (4 Bed)
• Site Plan:
o 2331-A005 (16) Ambrym St Proposed Subdivision – Lots + Houses
These documents define the design intent only and do not limit the Contractor’s obligations under this Contract.
Key Requirements
• The Contractor is responsible for the complete design development, coordination and certification of the Works.
• The Contractor shall ensure the Works comply with all applicable legislation, codes, standards and authority requirements.
• The Works shall be delivered as a complete and functional development, notwithstanding any omissions or discrepancies in the reference documentation.
• A mandatory Indigenous Employment and Business Participation (IEBP) Plan shall be submitted with the Tender. Failure to submit an IEBP may result in the Tender being deemed non-conforming.
Finished Floor Levels (FFL)
• Finished Floor Levels (FFL) has been provided and detailed on the below;
o ARO0319-C13 (SEWER) - C13
Scope of Works
Without limiting the Contractor’s obligations, the Works include but are not limited to:
Design & Approvals
• Detailed building designs of all dwellings and associated infrastructure
• Design coordination across all disciplines
• Preparation of all documentation required for construction, approvals and certification
o The Contractor must submit SD stage drawings to Dept. of Housing for feedback and approval prior to progressing to CD.
• Obtaining all approvals, permits and authority clearances
Site Establishment & Civil Works
• Site establishment, access, temporary services and controls
• Clearing, grubbing, demolition if required and site preparation (demolition of existing dwellings is excluded from this tender)
• Bulk earthworks, cut and fill, trimming and compaction incl. testing if applicable
• Set-out of all works in accordance with approved design and issued FFLs
• Stormwater drainage, overland flow management and lawful discharge
Residential Construction
• Construction of ten dwellings in accordance with nominated house types
• Masonry block construction to all dwellings
• All works to be in accordance with Remote Housing Standards (RHS) or as otherwise nominated
• Complete construction of each dwelling including all structural, architectural and building elements necessary to achieve a fully functional and compliant dwelling
• Supply and installation of all fixtures, fittings and appliances
• Kitchens, bathrooms and laundries including joinery
services & Utilities
• All hydraulic services including water, sewer and drainage
• electrical reticulation, metering, switchboards and internal installations
• Communications and data provisions as typically required
• Coordination and connection to mains
External & Ancillary Works
• Driveways, pathways, hardstand areas and access ways
• Fencing, gates and boundary treatments
• landscaping, reinstatement and making good of all disturbed areas
• Installation of external fixtures including T-Bar clotheslines, external sink and bins
Testing, Commissioning & Handover
• Testing and commissioning of all services and systems
• Rectification of defects and completion of outstanding works
• Provision of as-constructed documentation, manuals and warranties
• Final certification and approvals for occupancy
• Handover of a complete, operational and compliant development
Contractor Obligations
• The Contractor shall verify all site conditions, dimensions, levels and service locations prior to commencing Works.
• The Contractor shall be deemed to have allowed for all conditions that may reasonably be anticipated from available information and site inspection.
• No claim will be accepted for matters arising from failure to adequately investigate site conditions or coordinate the Works.
• The Contractor is responsible for all coordination, interfaces and integration of the Works, whether expressly stated or reasonably inferred.
Project Specification
• Design and Construction Standards for Remote Housing – August 2025
• National Construction Code (NCC)
The Contractor shall comply with the current edition of the National Construction Code (NCC), all applicable Australian Standards (AS/NZS), and all relevant statutory and regulatory requirements.
It shall be the Contractor’s responsibility to identify and comply with all standards applicable to the Works, whether they are specifically listed in this document.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Details: RFT 2026-004 - Whyalla Airport - water Main Upgrade (CCW058589)
Council is seeking to engage a suitably qualified contractor to undertake the supply and installation of approximately 1.5km water main from Lincoln Highway to Whyalla Airport facility. The purpose of this project is to:
• Replace the existing asbestos cement water main with a modern, non-asbestos pipe material
• Improve the reliability and efficiency of the local water supply network
• Reduce the risk of unplanned outages, leaks, and emergency repairs
• Minimise ongoing maintenance and lifecycle costs
• Eliminate asbestos-containing material from the water infrastructure in the project area
• Ensure compliance with current workplace health and safety and environmental requirements.

Details: P721 - KidSafe Connect Service Reimagination Strategy Partner (DCP058602)
The Department for Child Protection (DCP) is seeking, through this Invitation to Supply (ITS), a partner with proven expertise in service reimagination strategy, in complex, multi-stakeholder environments to ensure the KidSafe Connect (KSC) Program delivers high-quality, secure, and user-centred services.
The KidSafe Connect (KSC) Program is driving a major digital transformation to reimagine how child protection services are delivered and accessed. As part of this reimagining, the program remains committed to ensuring that DCP continues to safeguard children and families in all transformation enablers.
Procurement Objectives and Expected Outcomes
- Discovery and current state assessment
- Develop a Service Reimagination strategy
- Develop a practical implementation roadmap
- Define data, information flow and interoperability considerations
- Develop risk assessment and controls
- Improve customer and user experience capability.

Details: CTTG140 (CTTG058592). The City of Tea Tree Gully invites tenders to establish a Building maintenance Standing Offer panel.
Council utilises qualified contractors to undertake building maintenance on Council owned premises and land.
Council wishes to establish a panel of preferred Contractors to undertake building maintenance on Council owned premises, as detailed in the scope document.
The objective of this Panel is to ensure that:
· Council facilities are safe and maintained to a high standard.
· Council has a streamlined process for engaging contractors to deliver works and services, whilst still achieving value for money results.
· Council is able to undertake works in an efficient manner whilst ensuring all contracted providers have the appropriate understanding of WHS obligations, licenses, insurances, skills, capacity and capability to undertake work required and in accordance with Council’s requirements.
To achieve these outcomes, the panel will include a select number of contractors to enhance business efficiency and enable Council to form meaningful business relationships.

Clarence City Council invites Quotations from suitably qualified contractors to undertake clubroom renovation and cool room installation works at Lauderdale Oval, located at 10a Dona Road, Lauderdale, Tasmania.

This quotation seeks Respondents to supply all labour, materials, plant, and equipment to undertake:
• Clubroom entrance upgrades;
• Internal building modifications;

Agency: Strathbogie Shire Council
Location: VIC
Closing: 31/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown

Please call 1800 065 993 to make an appointment.
Details: Community Grants Program - Sustainability Grants Category
These grants support Council’s Climate Change Action Plan with a focus on energy saving initiatives at community managed, Council owned facilities, and reducing waste going to landfill. Projects must be completed in the 2026-27 financial year and may include, for
example, upgrades to energy efficient appliances, installation of renewable energy supply, bicycle repairs/recycling, or hard waste recovery projects. Sustainability Grant allocations will be determined by the Chief Executive Officer following assessment and applicants will be notified of the outcome early July. Applicants must provide a contribution to the project budget which can be cash or in-kind.
Maximum Grant Available: $20,000.

Details: CT001561 - Modular Sports Pavilion Panel Design, Fabrication and Installation Program 1 – Saltwater Pavilion
Council invites tenders from suitably qualified contractors for the establishment of a panel of prequalified suppliers for the design, supply, and installation of modular sports and community pavilions.
The panel will support the delivery of new and upgraded facilities across municipal and community assets, with a focus on efficient, scalable, and high-quality modular construction solutions.
Scope of services
The panel will cover, but is not limited to:
• Design and documentation of modular pavilion facilities
• Off-site fabrication and manufacturing
• Supply and installation of modular building components
• Civil, structural, and services integration (including site works)
• Compliance with relevant Australian Standards, National Construction Codes, and local authority requirements
• Sustainable and energy-efficient design solutions; and
• Project management, test and commissioning
Modular Projects may include sports pavilions, community hubs, changerooms, amenities buildings, and multipurpose facilities.
The initial term of the panel (Deed of Standing Offer) is five (5) years, with two (2) additional one (1) year extension options, exercisable at the sole discretion of the principal.

Event host (Victorian Seniors Festival)

Tenders.Net #: 1603013
Agency: Corangamite Shire Council
Location: VIC
Closing: 12/06/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Belinda Rowbottom
P: 5593 7100
E: servicenav@corangamite.vic.gov.au

Interested community groups or social clubs, made up of exclusively volunteers over 55 years, can call Belinda Rowbottom on 5593 7100 or email servicenav@corangamite.vic.gov.au to discuss their proposal or to learn more.
Details: Victorian Seniors Festival Event Host - Expressions of Interest
We're inviting groups in Corangamite Shire to join in the Victorian Seniors Festival 2026 by hosting an event to celebrate being a senior during the month of October.
Council is producing a brochure to promote events as part of the festival, and this is your opportunity to be involved.
The theme for 2026 is connect, create and celebrate which brings a positive message that encourages people to learn, connect with others and to live their best life!
We’d love to see you hold an event that will celebrate being an older person and encourage seniors in our community to participate and enjoy themselves. It’s about growing in experience, connecting with others and having some fun. It’s also a great way to let the community know about your group, which can be promoted as part of the Corangamite Shire Council’s Victorian Seniors Festival promotional campaign.
Your group are in control of the event and its planning, including the date, venue, catering, and any entertainment.
Council will help promote your event as part of the festival program.
Let us know about your event, or your proposed event, via the form (see link provided).
