Invite only: Senior Microsoft 365 application developer

Tenders.Net #: 1602992
Agency: Bureau of Meteorology
Location: VIC
Closing: 30/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Wednesday, 22 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06264 - 1 Senior Microsoft 365 Application developer
The Bureau seeks a contractor to design, implement, migrate, and operationalise a data management solution for the Flood Warning Infrastructure Network (FWIN) program. The solution will be implemented on Microsoft Power Apps + Microsoft Dataverse, with automation via Power Automate and integration hooks to GIS and Power BI, to improve data integrity, governance, user experience, auditability, and integration.
Background to Requirement
FWIN currently manages asset acquisition and lifecycle data using multiple SharePoint Lists. This approach has reached its limits, resulting in recurring issues with data quality, relational constraints, auditability, security, workflow orchestration, and integrations (notably breaks in Power BI reports and manual GIS export steps). A future-state evaluation compared:
Option 1: Power Apps + Dataverse
Option 2: Single consolidated SharePoint List
The analysis found Power Apps + Dataverse best meets FWIN’s needs for relational data structures, robust governance and security, workflow automation, scalability, auditability, and reliable analytics/GIS integrations. The contractor will deliver this future state and transition FWIN from SharePoint Lists to Dataverse as source of truth. This role will be located in the Bureau's Melbourne office.
Objectives and Outcomes
• Establish a Dataverse data model, configured security, and workflow automation to support FWIN lifecycle management.
• Improve data integrity with validated forms, enforced business rules and quality checks.
• Enable consistent, reliable integrations with GIS and Power BI.
• Provide an administered environment with audit history, role-based access, and clear governance.
• Deliver documentation, training and handover to internal staff.
Outcomes
• A single source of truth in Dataverse with robust relational integrity and field-level controls.
• controlled, guided user experience (Power App) that reduces errors and supports lifecycle workflows.
• Analytics through Power BI and an automated GIS export pattern (where feasible).
• Improved data governance: auditability, approvals, change control, and stewardship processes.
• Operational readiness: runbook, support model, and transferred knowledge.
Key duties and responsibilities
Full Requirements – Responsibilities
1. Comply with all Bureau work, health and safety policies and procedures, and take reasonable care for your own health and safety and that of employees, contractors and visitors who may be affected by your conduct.
2. Commitment to APS Values, Employment Principles, Code of Conduct and the Bureau's Social Justice Strategy.
3. The contractor will operate within the Bureau's M365/Power Platform environment and comply with all Bureau ICT security policies for OFFICIAL information. The contractor is required to hold a Baseline security clearance or ability to obtain one.
Knowledge and Skills Required
Functional Requirements
1. Data Model & Forms
- Implement a relational data model in Dataverse covering core FWIN entities (e.g., sites, assets, lifecycle states, metadata, reference data).
- Provide validated, guided forms with business rules, conditional logic, and field-level validation.
- Enforce mandatory fields by lifecycle stage; prevent invalid state transitions.
2. Governance, security & audit
- Implement role-based access control (RBAC) and field-level security (team/role based).
- Enable auditing (create/update/delete, security/role changes, configuration changes) and maintain version history for key records.
- Provide a change control approach for configuration.
3. Workflow & Automation
- Configure business process flows for lifecycle management.
- Implement scheduled data quality checks using Power Automate where appropriate.
- Configure notifications/approvals for key events (e.g., state changes, validation failures).
4. Integrations
- Establish reliable Power BI dataflows/datasets for analytics.
- Design and, where feasible in this engagement, implement an automated GIS export pattern to eliminate manual steps (or propose a staged approach).
5. Migration
- Plan and execute migration from current SharePoint Lists (FReD) to Dataverse, including data profiling, cleansing rules, mapping, and reconciliation.
6. Documentation & Handover
- Deliver solution design, data dictionary, security model, workflow specs, integration design, operational runbook (backup/restore, incident, access), and admin/user guides.
- Provide training (admin + user) and knowledge transfer sessions with recordings and quick reference guides.
Non-Functional Requirements
• security & Privacy: Align to Bureau ICT security policies for OFFICIAL data.
• Compliance: Support records/archives and applicable Australian Government policies and standards.
Key Performance Indicators
• Data Quality Pass Rate:100%
• Migration Reconciliation: 100%
• security & audit: 100% of privileged changes logged; 0 unauthorised access incidents attributable to solution design.
• Documentation & Handover: All artefacts delivered, reviewed, and accepted; = 85% training satisfaction (post-session survey).
• Delivery Timeliness: Milestones achieved within ±10% of agreed baseline schedule.
Standards
The Contractor must ensure the solution aligns with the following, as applicable to OFFICIAL information handled within the Bureau’s O365 environment.
Documentation
Contractor must produce and maintain (at minimum):
• Solution Design (current/future-state architecture, entity definitions, relationships, rules)
• security Model (roles, field-level security)
• Workflow & Automation Specs
• Integration Design (Power BI, GIS export pattern, interfaces)
• Migration Plan & Report (profiling, cleansing, mapping, test results, reconciliation outcomes)
• Operational Runbook (monitoring, backup/restore, incident & change procedures, access requests)
• User & Admin Guides; Training materials and recordings
• Test Plans and Evidence (functional, integration, performance, security)
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Details: LH-06260 - 1 Senior Product Software Engineer (Developer)
We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Senior Product Developer & SME to support and build upon a product generation framework based on Quadient's Inspire Flex product. The successful candidate will be responsible for supporting projects with the analysis required to feed into the design and build of templates, creating standard templates and patterns for future projects, providing technical expertise on Inspire Flex to projects and support teams, uplifting the BAU support team's skills to provide ongoing capability, deploying the software to production, and supporting projects in development and test. Additionally, the candidate should have technical and thought leadership skills to coach others and help develop their capability with this specialist software.
Key duties and responsibilities
The successful candidate will have responsibility to ensure the Automatic Weather Station Network meets the Bureau's current and future needs. To deliver this, the position holder will be broadly responsible to undertake the following duties:
1. Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of data acquisition (DAQ), remote terminal (RTU) or other monitoring equipment (operation, maintenance, and integration with other systems), particularly in relation to data ingestion and external control mechanisms.
2. Review, problem find and maintain software and scripts in Python and C++, in alignment with established development processes.
3. Support the planning and execution of equipment testing activities to ensure reliability and performance.
4. Interpret strategic objectives and outcomes, identify potential challenges and uncertainties, and incorporate these insights into planning, decision-making, and implementation processes.
5. Collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders, fostering clear communication and positively influencing team outcomes.
6. Assess delivered equipment and systems to ensure compliance with technical requirements, legislation, policies, and regulatory standards.
7. Review activities to identify success factors and actively contribute to continuous improvement and innovation initiatives.
Knowledge and Skills Required
Mandatory
Extensive technical and hands-on experience in the development of complex monitoring systems, incorporating:
Embedded micro-controller data acquisition and processing systems, focusing on software development in high level programming/scripting languages, Python – mandatory and C++ advantageous.
Strong understanding of sensor technologies, interfacing methods, and custom measurement systems.
LINUX system administration relevant to embedded/field hardware, backend systems and sensors.
Demonstrated proficiency in software engineering, with expertise across the full software development lifecycle (requirements analysis, specification development, implementation, verification, and review).
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent
Desirable
• Creation and execution of test plans, code management, and performance analysis and reporting.
• In depth understanding of communications technologies (Serial, Ethernet, analogue, RF), data transmission protocols (TCP Sockets, FTP, MQTT, SNMP) and remote communications (modem, cellular, satellite).
• Development and construction of prototypes, including bench and field based implementations.
• Proven capability in designing, implementing, and maintaining systems that support accurate and reliable observations
Essential criteria
Technical competence
• Demonstrates the right skills and experience, a good understanding of the requirements, and works in an agile way.
• Technical competence is also assessed by reviewing the candidates mandatory and desirable skills and experience.
Experience
• Demonstrated previous experience in a similar role.
• Delivers on intended results and outcomes.
Capability
• Availability and capacity to undertake the role.
• Ability to develop and contribute to innovative solutions.
• Demonstrated ability to make decisions, involve others as appropriate, and resolve issues.
Cultural contribution
• How well the candidate will contribute to the organisation's culture.
• Candidate's values and behaviour are similar to the organisations.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Notes: Online - Microsoft Teams meetingJoin: https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/49430114140277?p=mAsI1ct2PeThNXE6L3Meeting ID: 494 301 141 402 77Passcode: 27zJ2RZ6Need help? | System referenceDial in by phone+61 2 8318 0090,,784528129# Australia, SydneyFind a local numberPhone conference ID: 784 528 129#

Details: FOL/25/7998 - WorkSafe Victoria Employee Assistance Program
Work Safe Victoria is seeking the services of a suitably qualified organisation(s) to provide an Employee Assistance Program, Critical incident, and proactive Early Intervention services at all sites and locations of work.
WorkSafe is seeking responses for the delivery of these services from October 2026, for an initial three-year term, with the option for extension by a further three-year term.
Service - Employee Assistance Program (EAP) / Critical Incident (CI)
EAP - WorkSafe’s EAP aims to assist and support WorkSafe employees reduce stress by resolving or working through personal, family and/or workplace issues. The EAP is a short term, confidential, professional, psychological counselling service, typically initiated by the employee. Employees retain responsibility for their own treatment and other choices. EAP services include telephone, online and face to face counselling options.
CI - CI services are based on early intervention principles, to support challenging workplace events that have a probability of causing employee distress.
Service - Early Intervention (EI)
EI - programs are proactive psychological coaching services to employees in teams at risk of cumulative trauma. They are considered best practice controls in high-risk settings where employees have frequent exposure to traumatic events and content, violence and aggression, contacts in distress or contacts who demonstrate unreasonable and vexatious behaviours.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Contact: Interested parties are encouraged to urgently contact Keagan Driscoll on (03) 8663 6230 or keagan.driscoll@au.gt.com to discuss this opportunity and to obtain further information.
Details: Expressions of Interest Sought - Sale of Business
Rihaan SDP Pty Ltd Trading as IGA Meadow Heights
(Administrators Appointed)
Mott Byrnes and Andrew Hewitt of Grant Thornton were appointed Joint and Several Administrators of Rihaan SDP Pty Ltd Trading as IGA Meadow Heights ("the Company") on 8 April 2026.
The Joint and Several Administrators are currently seeking urgent expressions of interest regarding an opportunity to acquire the Company's IGA Meadow Heights supermarket business as a going concern.
The IGA Meadow Heights supermarket operates within an established and prominent local shopping precinct, which is located within a suburban community in the northern suburbs of Melbourne.
The Administrators wish to note that the Company trades under the IGA Brand in accordance with a trademark and license agreement.
This may result in approval from Metcash being required in a scenario where the IGA brand is utilised by a purchaser post-acquisition.

Vegetation & hazard removal

Tenders.Net #: 1602784
Agency: Yarriambiack Shire Council
Location: VIC
Closing: 14/05/2026 - 02:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Opportunity contact & details updated.

Yarriambiack Shire Council - Municipal Office
34 Lyle Street
PO Box 243
Warracknabeal VIC 3393
P: 03 5398 0100 / 1800 065 647
F: 03 5398 2502
E: info@yarriambiack.vic.gov.au

Any questions or requests for clarification or further information regarding this Request for Tender must be directed to Council via the e-procurement portal Questions tab.
Details: C372-2026 - Fire Prevention - Vegetation & Hazard Removal
Yarriambiack Shire Council is seeking the services of an experienced Contractor to assist in the removal of identified fire hazards across the municipality on private land, as per the Country Fire Authority Act 1958 and the Local Government Act 2020.
The Contractor must be available to complete fire hazard removal works from late October until early March; works will include but not be limited to:
Slashing, mowing, mulching, brush cutting and trimming of grass and weeds on private property (in both urban and rural areas);
Removal of hazards including trees, weeds, limbs, branches, timber, rubbish, etc.
The services to be provided under this contract are located in various townships in the municipality, from Tempy in the north to Lubeck in the south; the location of each property and specific site instructions will be supplied to the Contractor by the Municipal Fire Prevention Officer via an app (Crisisworks).
The Contractor must undertake and complete the Fire Clearance Works for a property within seven (7) days of a written Works Request being issued by Council.
The Contractor shall, during the term of this contract, provide all necessary labour, plant and equipment to complete the work required.
Pricing is based on a set hourly rate, irrespective of equipment used and should take into consideration travel, plant and equipment and any other costs associated with providing the service.
The contact will be awarded for an initial three year term with a further two x one year options (up to a maximum term of five years).

A mandatory briefing session will be held at Mount Beauty WWTP, 33 Embankment Drive, Mount Beauty, Victoria, Australia, 3699 on 16 April 2026 11:00 am. Attendance at the briefing is a pre-requisite to submission and consideration of a bid. Registration closes on 16 April 2026 11:00 am.
Details: CT10515. Mount Beauty WWTP Switchboard & Motor control Centre Upgrade.
North East water is seeking to engage a suitably qualified Principal Contractor to install a new main switchboard and upgrade the MCC at the WWTP. The replacement of the MCC will incorporate an upgrade of the current Allen Brady PLCs to Omron NX Series PLCs.
The scope of works will include:
• Installation of new MSB including relocation.
• Installation of new MCC including upgrade of site PLCs to Omron NX Series.
• SCADA programming based on Liquitek’s “Functional Description”.
• Upgrade and relocation of Ausnet “point of supply” to the new MSB.
• Replacement of power, sub mains and control cabling.
The proposed project will be completed under a “Design and Construct” contract and is scheduled to be completed by March 2027.

Community inspired festive banners / public artwork

Tenders.Net #: 1603038
Agency: Shire of Augusta-Margaret River
Location: WA
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Please address any queries or requests for information to Tanya Perdue, Arts & Economic Development Coordinator on (08) 9780 5231 or tperdue@amrshire.wa.gov.au

Expressions of Interest can either be submitted by email to:
tperdue@amrshire.wa.gov.au with the subject line EOI Submission - Main Street Christmas Banner Artwork

OR

Sent as a hard copy in a sealed envelope, addressed as follows:
EOI Submission - Main Street Christmas Banner Artwork
Attention: Tanya Perdue
Arts & Economic Development Coordinator
Shire of Augusta Margaret River
PO Box 61
Margaret River WA 6285
Details: EOI Now Open for Artist to Create Community Inspired Festive Banners
Exciting opportunity for a local artist
Local artists are invited to apply for an exciting opportunity to create high-visibility public artwork in the heart of Margaret River
The Shire of Augusta Margaret River is calling for Expressions of Interest from local creatives to design a new series of banners that celebrate the festive season in a way that is meaningful to our community.
The project will see the successful artist run one to two creative workshops with school aged children, with their ideas brought together in professionally finished festive banners celebrating the Augusta Margaret River region.
The final artworks will be installed along Margaret River’s main street in the lead-up to Christmas and throughout the peak tourist season.
This initiative will help create a vibrant streetscape during the holiday season, in a way that reflects our people and place to foster community spirit and a sense of connection, joy, and togetherness.
It’s also a fantastic opportunity for a local artist to gain exposure during the busiest time of the year.
---
Scope of artwork
The scope of this project is to create a unique suite of 2-3 original banner artworks, to be reproduced and displayed throughout the Margaret River main street during the festive holiday season, for a period of up to five years.
As part of the creative process, the commissioned artist will be required to plan and deliver 1-2 creative workshops with school aged children. These workshops will generate visual material that is to be meaningfully and recognisably incorporated into the final, professionally resolved artworks, ensuring participating children can identify their creative contributions once the banners are installed.
The artist will be responsible for all aspects of workshop delivery, including facilitation, materials, associated costs and digitisation of artwork for inclusion in the final designs.
The Margaret River Zone Room can be utilised as a workshop venue for no additional cost, pending availability.
A total of 28 banners are located along the main street. The selected 2-3 designs will be reproduced across these banners. The artworks must be complementary in style, colour, and overall design, with the final digital design file provided in line with the provided template (as outlined in attachment Avia link provided). The arrangement of banners along the main street (e.g., alternating, in blocks, colour transition or other) can be proposed by the artist to best display the artworks as a street wide installation.
The artist is responsible for delivering finished artwork using supplied template/s. The Shire is responsible for banner production and installation.
5. Budget The fee available for the suite of two to three artworks is $4,500 ex GST.
There is an additional allowance of $2,500 ex GST to develop and run children’s creative workshops to engage children in the artistic process and to gain elements for incorporation into design work.

Details: PROMSA26060 - Pulse Energised Fence - Hakea Prison
Programmed facility management Pty Ltd (PFM) on behalf of Department of Housing and Works (DHW) and Department of Justice (DOJ) are seeking tenders from suitably qualified and experienced contractors to undertake Pulse Energised Fence Works at Hakea Prison
The Work includes but is not limited to the supply, installation, cabling, configuration and testing of a new E Fence detection system.

Tenders.Net #: 1602975
Agency: Department of Education
Location: WA
Closing: 08/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: For more information about the Supporting School Canteens program, phone 9264 4824 or email SupportingSchoolCanteens@education.wa.edu.au
Details: Supporting School Canteens Program Guidelines
The Supporting School Canteens program aims to enhance the delivery of nutritious and affordable meals to students.
The program offers funding for school canteen projects including the:
• purchase of equipment
• upgrade of canteen facilities.
Funding will be offered from 2026 to 2028 through an expression of interest program.
Round 1 applications are now open.
Funding requests
To ensure equitable distribution of funding and to maximise impact, the program has 2 funding streams:
• small projects up to $50,000, excluding GST
• large projects from $50,000 to $250,000, excluding GST.
• Funding requests cannot exceed $250,000, excluding GST.

Evaluating triage & deconfliction practices in countering violent extremism contexts

Tenders.Net #: 1602974
Agency: Western Australia Police Force
Location: WA
Closing: 15/05/2026 - 02:30 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: Jayden Weeratunga
P: (08) 65511810
E: jayden.weeratunga@dohw.wa.gov.au

An online briefing session will be held on 28 Apr, 2026 10:30 AM.
Link to join the meeting can be found in the Request under 1.5 Briefing, Location.
Or alternatively, via the link provided.

The Respondent is requested to confirm its attendance by no later than 2:30PM AWST Friday 24 April 2026, by contacting Jayden Weeratunga at jayden.weeratunga@dohw.wa.gov.au
Details: WAPOL15125 - Evaluating Triage and Deconfliction Practices for Mixed, Unclear, and Unstable Youth in Countering Violent Extremism Contexts
The Western Australia Police Force requires a qualified organisation to support improvements to its triage and deconfliction processes for individuals who may present grievance-fuelled or ideologically motivated risks. Clear and consistent practices between the WA Fixated Threat Assessment Centre (WAFTAC) and the WA Intervention and Support Program (WAISP) are essential to ensuring that individuals are appropriately directed to the most suitable intervention pathway.
To assist with this, the Western Australia Police Force seeks a supplier to undertake a comparative evaluation of current practices and provide practical policy guidance. The services will include research, analysis of existing processes, stakeholder engagement, and the preparation of a policy recommendations report supported by a validation workshop and final revised report.
The WAFTAC and WAISP teams are located at the Maylands Police Complex, 21 Swan BankRoad, Maylands WA 6051.

Artist opportunity for workshops

Tenders.Net #: 1602932
Agency: Shire of Carnarvon
Location: WA
Closing: Not Stated
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Artists interested in participating can submit an EOI to:
E: robertson.k@carnarvon.wa.gov.au
Details: EOI Calling First Nations Artists
First Nations Artists Invited to Lead Workshops at Gnulli Festival Cultural Activity Hub
First Nations artists and creatives are invited to take part in a paid opportunity to share their skills and cultural practices at the upcoming Gnulli Festival.
The Community Art Hub will be hosting a dedicated space at the festival called the Cultural Activity Hub, featuring a series of short, hands-on workshops delivered by local artists.
Opportunity details:
Event: Gnulli Festival - Cultural Activity Hub
Date: Saturday 23 May
Time: 8:30am-1:30pm (1-hour workshop time slots available)
Location: Cultural Activity Hub station, Gnulli Festival
Payment: Paid artist opportunity
Workshops can include a wide range of creative practices such as painting, weaving, jewellery making, storytelling through art, and other cultural or contemporary art forms. Artists are encouraged to design sessions that reflect their own practice and knowledge.
This is an opportunity to share culture and creativity with the community in a hands-on, interactive festival setting.

Oil field, facilities & infrastructure decommissioning phase (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1602894
Agency: Chevron Australia Pty Ltd
Location: WA
Closing: 01/02/2027
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Please confirm details & closing date of this opportunity with contact.

Hengameh Kiani
P: +61 08 9365 7543
E: hengameh.kiani@icnwa.org.au
Details: asset Retirement
Chevron Australia Pty Ltd (Chevron Australia) is operator of the Barrow Island Joint Venture (known as WA Oil), located on Barrow Island off the northwest coast of Western Australia (WA).
Since WA Oil production commenced in 1967, onshore production wells have gathered oil from the field beneath Barrow Island. Processing occurred on-island before oil was transported via pipeline to offshore tankers for export.
In 2025, the Barrow Island oil field ceased production when the last production well and production station were shut-in and isolated.
As a mature asset, the Barrow Island oil field and related facilities and infrastructure have now entered the decommissioning phase.
For more details, visit the Chevron Australia website.
===
WORK PACKAGES
Contract - EOI Close Date
Any Opportunities - 01 Feb 2027.

Processing plant - expansion project (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1599462
Agency: Pilgangoora Operations Pty Ltd
Location: WA
Closing: 25/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Please confirm details & closing date of this opportunity with contact.
NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 20/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity details & work packages last updated on 21/04/2026.

Joelle Lim
P: +61 08 9365 7623
E: joelle.lim@icnwa.org.au
Details: P2000 Expansion Project
Overview
PLS owns and operates the world's largest, independent hard rock lithium operation, the Pilgangoora Operation, located 120 kilometres from Port Hedland.
The P2000 Project, is the proposed expansion of Pilgangoora which aims to increase production of spodumene concentrate to approximately 2 million tonnes each year.
This expansion will be delivered through the design and construction of a new processing plant. The proposed flowsheet includes crushing and ore sorting, a wholeofore comminution circuit, preflotation magnetic separation and a tantalum byproduct recovery circuit.
The new facility will be constructed adjacent to the existing Pilgan Plant to leverage established services, processing facilities, infrastructure, and operational labour synergies.
PLS will publish all major tenders associated with the P2000 Project on the ICN WA Gateway in accordance with the Project schedule.
We are committed to ensuring that local suppliers are provided with full, fair, and reasonable opportunities to participate in the Project. Pilbara-based businesses are encouraged to add their contact details to our Pilbara Business Register to be notified when contracts are published.
Pilgangoora Operations Pty Ltd (POPL) is a wholly owned subsidiary of PLS Group Limited (PLS).
===

Brand tracking research

Tenders.Net #: 1595269
Agency: Western Australian Tourism Commission
Location: WA
Closing: 12/05/2026 - 02:30 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 21/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

Simone Surin
P: (08) 92621391
E: simone.surin@dohw.wa.gov.au
Details: TWA2604 - Brand Tracking Research
The Western Australian Tourism Commission, trading as Tourism Western Australia (Tourism WA; the Customer) is a statutory authority within the Department of Creative Industries, Tourism and Sports (CITS) and is governed by a Board of Commissioners. The purpose of Tourism WA is to promote and develop Western Australia's iconic tourism experiences. 
Tourism WA is seeking the services of one Contractor to provide Brand Tracking Research (BTR) services comprising evidence-based consumer insights that will inform Tourism WA's marketing strategy, campaign development and evaluate performance. 
This Request includes a Participation Plan as part of the Qualitative Requirements. Respondents must ensure a copy of the completed Participation Plan is submitted with the Offer.

Infrastructure project (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1472219
Agency: BHP Iron Ore Pty Ltd
Location: WA
Closing: 06/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Please confirm details & closing date of this opportunity with contact.
NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 23/08/2024, 13/09/2024, 14/10/2024, 15/10/2024, 28/10/2024, 31/10/2024, 04/11/2024, 11/11/2024, 15/11/2024, 12/09/2025, 09/03/2026 & 02/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity details & work packages last updated on 21/04/2026.

Joelle Lim
P: +61 08 9365 7623
E: joelle.lim@icnwa.org.au
Details: Ministers North Project
Overview
BHP is undertaking a study to understand requirements around the development of Ministers North (MN); a high-grade, Brockman ore deposit located, ~13km south-east of Yandi in the Central Pilbara.
As part of the project study, the Company is seeking expressions of interest (EOI) from suitably qualified contractors to gain an understanding of the capacity and capability of available contractors for the proposed project.
An Engineering, Procurement, Construction management (EPCM) contracting model has been selected for the MN project. The EPCM contractor will act as agent for Company, for the supply of some goods and services.
The Project scope is comprised of all facilities and infrastructure (permanent and temporary) required to support the potential MN development.
The scope to be delivered can be characterised across the following categories:
1. Greenfield - Mine, and associated Infrastructure
A new mine at MN;
Construction of a combined use Light Vehicle (LV) /Surface Mining Equipment (SME) haul road.
Dual lane LV/SME tunnel arch structure.
New level crossing over an existing rail line provided for construction access, prior to completion of the bridge.
water supply pipelines from supply location to MN and from MN to Yandi;
New fuel storage and refuelling including associated facilities at MN;
Communications networks including digital radio, internet and cellular services; and
Temporary construction infrastructure (including offices, warehousing, lay-downs, water and workshop).
2. Brownfield - Modifications and tie-ins
New primary and secondary crushing facilities and associated screening facilities at Yandi including partial demolition of existing eastern front-end facility.
Modifications to existing Yandi infrastructure (including overland conveyor, Coarse Ore Stockpile, scalping screens, tertiary crushers, product screens and conveyors) to produce fines products.
Relocation of existing power line to avoid the MN orebody.
Conversion of existing Train Loadout facility (TLO2).
Modifications to the existing reclaimer, yard conveyor and transfer station.
Upgrades to existing Yandi explosives storage facilities; and
Extension of raw, fire, and potable water services at primary and secondary crushing facilities.
3. Other Non-Process Infrastructure to support the new operation
Administration offices, first aid, crib rooms and ablution facilities at MN, including associated potable water, sewerage, and oily water systems.
maintenance bays and fuel facilities at MN;
Re-use and expansion of the existing Yandi Mobile Equipment workshop facilities; and
Tie-ins to existing power, raw water, and communications networks.
---
WORK PACKAGES
Contract - Full Scope Closes
Transport and Logistics services - 06 May 2026

Invite only: Senior ICT / cyber security officer

Tenders.Net #: 1602991
Agency: Australian Digital Health Agency
Location: ALL
Closing: 28/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Friday, 24 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06263 - 1 Senior ICT/Cyber security Officer
The Cyber Project Coordinator is accountable under limited direction to perform complex work to provide oversight over the Agency’s Cyber Solutions team engagements to ensure that work completed by the team is appropriately managed and engagements are successful.
This will include coordinating cyber security projects in a multi-vendor environment, creating and managing integrated project schedules that align dependencies and deliverables, track progress and minimise project risk.
The Project Coordinator has a sufficient level of technical knowledge to understand technical discussions and directions in order to communicate information to required stakeholders, some of which may be non-technical.
Key duties and responsibilities
1. Leadership and Accountability
Manage the delivery of cyber security work packages for Agency projects including security requirements definition, integrating security testing schedules, aligning dependencies and tracking progress.
Acquire, maintain and apply an in-depth knowledge of the Agency’s project management framework, methodologies and activities required to coordinate and support the Agency’s project and reporting requirements.
Provide the coordination and support to ensure an Agency’s project is compliant with the assurance and governance processes for policy, security, clinical safety, and design; monitoring progress, gathering information to produce monthly project status reports and other key project documentation.
Accountable to provide a high level of support to other Agency project managers to develop and maintain project risk and issue registers; identifying and reporting variances and issues; providing assistance and coordination for risk mitigation, response and treatment actions.
Manage contracts, service agreements and purchase orders; monitor expenditure and emerging issues that may impact on Agency priorities engaging with risk and undertaking risk management activities for area of responsibility, ensuring compliance and delivery of outcomes.
2. management Diversity and Span
Coordinate and provide support to monitor, track and report on the status of project, program deliverables to ensure schedules, cost and quality metrics are in line with approved project plans.
3. Job Context and Environment
Conducting reviews to ensure risks and issues related to projects, programs under their responsibility are identified, assessed and mitigated and are in alignment with applicable Agency frameworks.
Manage the delivery of security testing services through multiple external vendors.
Define, plan, and execute the plans to achieve and maintain security certification requirements for the Agency. Collaborate with and manage the deliverables across the Cyber Solutions team for cyber security architects, risk leads and assurance and compliance leads.
Provide a range of administration, project, program, support services to facilitate the delivery of outcomes within the Branch.
Work with teams, partners and/or clients to track progress and compliance against the requirements contained in detailed program, project, governance, communication and reporting plans.
4. Independence and decision-making
Work with direction from Project management Office, against established Agency project and operational priorities, framework and methodologies to deliver quality outcomes ensuring project tools, templates and processes are adhered to.
Support in activities to understand business needs and assist the Project management Office in documenting and implementing best practice processes to assist in successful delivery of technology projects.
Research, anticipate and analyse activities to identify business needs, particularly those that may be conflicting. Manage and negotiate resolutions to complex or sensitive issues or use judgement to escalate.
Co-ordinate the preparation of reports and briefing papers for executives to support evidence-based decision making
5. Stakeholder management
Manage relationships with internal and external stakeholders to achieve project(s), operations, work area and agency goals; anticipating and responding to their needs and expectations ensuring stakeholder expectations are clearly articulated, documented and understood by the project delivery team.
Communicate and negotiate with stakeholders; working under limited direction to deal with complex and contentious matters requiring persuasion and sensitivity to minimize oppositions and maximise acceptance and cooperation.
Essential criteria
1. Relevant experience managing multiple projects and security activities in a large Organisation. Forecasting branch wide resource requirements and executing government procurement processes.
2. Relevant experience in managing delivery of cyber security work packages/projects.
3. Relevant knowledge of cyber security project deliverables e.g. security requirements definition, security testing types.
4. Relevant experience in delivery of security testing services through multiple external vendors.
5. Relevant project management experience to deliver technical projects.
6. Proven record of managing projects that achieve/uphold security certifications such as ISO27001 and ISM

Details: 108664. Specialist legal services for small businesses.
For the ASBFEO, a specialist legal practitioner engaged from the list will be required to provide eligible unrepresented small business clients with advice at two stages of the dispute resolution process (noting there will be flexibility in the service as not all matters will be subject to both stages).
The current Guidelines for the service are available at
https://www.asbfeo.gov.au/disputes-assistance/access-low-cost-advice-disputes
Note these guidelines are for the pilot and are subject to change, which will include an increase in the time offered to small businesses for the service, as reflected in the service requirement in this ATM.
Legal advice may be provided in-person, or via phone or videoconferencing. The specialist legal practitioner is responsible for providing facilities for meetings.
See document link for full details of this opportunity.
Timeframe for Delivery: 7 August 2026 to 6 August 2030.

Services & materials for antifoam package - chemical injection skid

Tenders.Net #: 1602934
Agency: Shell Australia
Location: ALL
Closing: 18/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: For further details, please refer to the link provided.
Details: EOI-00247 - Provision of services and Materials for Antifoam Package – Chemical Injection Skid
The scope relates to the following Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) supplied equipment manufactured by Milton Roy:
• Antifoam Package (1 unit)
• Chemical Injection Skids (2 units)
• Portable Chemical Injection Package (1 unit)
The services sought through this EOI are integral to ensuring the continued safe, reliable, and efficient operation of these systems.
Detailed Scope of services
Supervision and maintenance services
Respondents are expected to demonstrate capability to provide:
· Specialist supervision services for the identified equipment packages where service intervals are six (6) months or more;
· maintenance service labour for routine and non‑routine maintenance activities where service intervals are six (6) months or more;
· Completion of service activities in accordance with OEM recommendations and COMPANY requirements; and
· Provision of service documentation upon completion of activities.
Where service frequencies are less than six (6) months, such activities are typically performed by COMPANY maintenance personnel.
Materials and Consumables Support
Respondents shall demonstrate capability to:
· Support the use of Company‑supplied operational spares issued via COMPANY work orders, including return of unused or replaced items;
· Supply low‑value consumables where items are not Company‑supplied, to facilitate service and maintenance activities;
· Manage consumables in a controlled and auditable manner.
Bulk procurement of equipment or materials is out of scope for this EOI.
Technical and OEM Support
Respondents shall demonstrate capability to:
· Provide technical support for engineering and operational queries related to Milton Roy equipment;
· Escalate issues to the OEM where required; and
· Support troubleshooting, diagnostics, and technical advice aligned with OEM standards.
Training Support
Respondents shall demonstrate capability to:
· Deploy service personnel with appropriate OEM or equivalent certifications where practical;
· Provide on‑board refresher or familiarisation support to COMPANY personnel to maintain operational knowledge of the equipment.
Training provided does not replace COMPANY’s competency management framework.

Short course - mobilising capital through insurance & pension reform

Tenders.Net #: 1602861
Agency: Tetra Tech International Development
Location: ALL
Closing: 22/05/2026 - 03:00 pm (ACST South Australian Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Janne Laukkala
Position Professional and Leadership Development Adviser
E: Tendersubmissions@tetratech.com

Last Queries Date: Monday 11 May 2026 at 3:00pm (ACST South Australian time)

Closing date for Registration of Intent to submit a tender: Monday 4 May 2026 at 3:00pm (ACST South Australian time)
Details: RFT AM 13547 - Australia Awards in Indonesia - Mobilising Long-Term Capital through Insurance and Pension Reform in Indonesia Short Course
Tetra Tech International Development, the Managing Contractor for the Australia Awards in Indonesia (AAI) is seeking tenders for the design and delivery of Mobilising Long-Term Capital through Insurance and Pension Reform in Indonesia short course.
Tenders are sought in accordance with the AAI Short Course Guidelines and as per the details more fully described in the Request for Tender (RFT) documentation.
Details of the course are included in Part B – Scope of services.
To tender to design and deliver this course the lead business must be a Registered Training Organisation (RTO) in Australia or an Australian tertiary institution as defined in the Higher Education Support Act, 2003 Table A, B and C. AAI will exclude a tenderer on the grounds of bankruptcy, insolvency, false declarations, or significant deficiencies in performance of any substantive requirement or obligation under a prior contract with Tetra Tech International Development or AAI.
This short course consists of a 3-day pre-course workshop in Indonesia, a 2-week course in Australia, 3-4 online mentoring sessions, and a 3-day post-course workshop in Indonesia.
The final delivery dates and locations will be determined in consultation with DFAT and the course provider. The proposed schedule (dates may change based on course provider’s availability):
• Pre-course workshop: August 2026
• Short course in Australia: September 2026
• Post-course workshop: January or February 2027
A 2-hour online briefing will be conducted jointly by the preferred tenderer and AAI approximately one month before departure. Date will be negotiated with the preferred tenderer.
The course provider will offer 3-4 online Award Project mentoring sessions (2-3 hours per session). Two or three of these sessions take place between the in-Australia course and the post-course workshop to ensure the participants are supported to finalise their Award Projects by the post-course workshop.

Head of PACER Plus implementation unit

Tenders.Net #: 1603058
Agency: Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade
Location: NZ
Closing: 12/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Samoa -

http://www.gets.govt.nz/
Details: Samoa
---
RFx ID : 33950739 - Head of the PACER Plus Implementation Unit (Reference # : WPG-0105505)
The Pacific Agreement on Closer Economic Relations (PACER) Plus is a landmark trade and development agreement that has been signed by 11 Forum Island Countries: Australia, Cook Islands, Kiribati, Nauru, New Zealand, Niue, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu. The Agreement covers goods, services, and investment. It aims to lower barriers to trade, provide greater certainty for businesses, raise living standards, create jobs and increase exports across the Pacific.
The Parties to PACER Plus are looking to appoint a suitably qualified and experienced person to work as the Head of the Implementation Unit reporting to the PACER Plus Joint Committee. The key objectives of this senior level role are to provide critical leadership and technical expertise on trade and development for the PACER Plus Implementation Unit.
Working closely with the PACER Plus Parties and signatories, you will be required to build positive and productive relationships across the Pacific region. You will be politically astute and recognise the importance of stakeholder engagement; be an effective and motivated leader; be highly organised, have self-motivated structured programme and reporting work habits; and understand and respect the culture and diversity of the environment.
This role will appeal if you are interested in a position that contributes to the prosperity and resilience of the people of the Pacific. The role will be for a period of 5 years. The successful candidate will be able to take up the position in Samoa.

Wilding project management

Tenders.Net #: 1602988
Agency: Southland Regional Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 11/05/2026 - 12:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/
Details: RFx ID: 33953787 - Mid Dome Wilding Project management (Reference #: C26/01)
The Mid Dome wilding conifer programme has been running for over 20 years with the Mid Dome Wilding Tree Charitable Trust (The Trust). During this time The Trust has employed a Project Manager to oversee the operational work. Due to recent changes with The trust taking more of a governance role, Environment Southland is now overseeing the contract and are looking for a competent company to undertake the Project management and operational work within the Flagstaff and Mid Dome areas.
The National Wilding Conifer control Programme led by the Ministry of Primary Industries aims to prevent the spread of wilding pines and to progressively remove them from the land already invaded. 'The right tree in the right place: The New Zealand Wilding Conifer management Strategy 2015-2030' (Strategy) provides the framework for the national programme. Mid Dome and Flagstaff are management Units covered by the National Wilding Conifer Programme.
This programme is funded through the National Wilding Conifer control programme and contributions from The Trust. As this is locally and nationally funded there is currently no guaranteed funding going forward though indications for 2026/27 are looking promising and future funding is hoped to be clarified by early 2027. At present it is looking unlikely that we will be able to fully fund the 2023-2033 Strategy commissioned by The Trust and are going back to the basic ideas of The Trust’s original strategy with priorities being to maintain the cleared areas, control original seed sources and prevent secondary spread.
The Mid Dome operational wilding area consists of just under 30,000 hectares of wilding conifers. This is a mix of tenure of Crown land, conservation land and pastural land. The predominant species are Pinus Contorta (est 90%) with an unknown amount of mugo and an increasing amount of Douglas fir potentially coming from outside the programme area.

Airport resilience project (various works)

Tenders.Net #: 1602377
Agency: Westland District Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 29/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/

NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 15/05/2026. Please confirm with contact.
Details: RFx ID : 33935398 - WDC Hokitika Airport Resilience Project - HARP
The Hokitika Airport Resilience Project (HARP) aims to increase the overall resilience of the airport by improving the airports’ ability to support regional airline aircraft at higher weights and passenger loadings in a wider range of weather conditions and natural hazard events and improve the ability of the region to respond more effectively to natural hazard events. In doing this, WDC hope to improve the safety and security of the Hokitika Airport, protect the airfield assets, and increase its long-term maintainability.
WDC are seeking to engage a Contractor to successfully deliver the required civil works, detailed in the design documents.
The successful contractor will need to be successfully deliver:
• The extension works for the Main 03-21 runway Starter Extensions, through the construction of a high-quality granular pavement and asphaltic concrete surfacing.
• The resealing works for the Main 03-21 runway, with the overlay of asphaltic concrete (AC) to the full runway length and width.
• The resealing works for the existing 12-30 runway, between the taxiway and 20 thresholds, using Grade 5 chipseal and sand seal treatment.
• The required apron extension works, through the construction of a high-quality granular pavement and asphaltic concrete surfacing and installation of a new oil separator.
• The required drainage works, which include bulk earthworks and shaping in the runway strip, installation of new drainage pipework, rehabilitation of existing sumps and various remedials.
• Resealing works for the Taxiway from the main runway to Apron.
In doing this, the contractor must also:
• Be willing to work collaboratively with ourselves and our design team to deliver a solution that meets the requirements of the Hokitika Airport.
• Be familiar with all the processes and procedures associated with working airside.
• Be able to mobilise to undertake the works from 1 September 2026.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Budgeting & forecasting system

Tenders.Net #: 1598510
Agency: Thames-Coromandel District Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 24/04/2026 - 04:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/

NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 21/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.
Details: RFx ID : 33819438 - RFP for Budgeting and Forecasting System
The Principal are seeking suitably qualified suppliers to submit a Proposal to enable the Principal to replace set of complex and bespoke spreadsheets as their primary planning, budget and forecasting tool for an integrated off the shelf product that performs the core budget and forecasting functions expected of a modern District Council.
This includes the capability to determine and calculate Loans (both internal and external) and Development Contributions as well as the capability to incorporate the recent changes to legislative changes to water infrastructure (Local water Done Well) to ensure our finances align to the Disclosure Determination set out by the Commerce Commission.
Also in scope is the requirement to build and publish reports and dashboards. These would include standard reports to aid operational and strategic decisions (up to 30 years) as well as key accountability documents such as the Annual Plan, Long Term Plan and Annual Report which include Financial Impact Statements.
The new system must be able to import actual data from our other systems (Payroll System, Microsoft Dynamics and Monday.com and Universe (AMS). Import data includes both financial and non-financial (by way of example Salary costs as well as FTE numbers). While the new system must be able to accommodate actual financial data at General Ledger level (the Principal are not seeking any to change our existing general ledger structure) the Principal are seeking to transfer five years of financial history from our existing budget system into the new system.
Our expectation is that the next Long Term Annual Plan will be developed using the new Budgeting and Forecasting System.

Closing: 15/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/

NOTE: Opportunity details updated.

A supplier briefing will be held. Please register via the link provided to attend this session.
Details: RFx ID: 33749619 - Very high temperature heat pump activator initiative - energy efficiency demonstration projects utilising EECA co-funding support - Service Providers (Reference #: VHTHPA for Service Providers)
EECA’s Very High Temperature Heat Pump (VHTHP) Activator demonstration initiative focuses on HTHPs, which supply process heat very efficiently, with conversion efficiencies in the 300% to 500% range.
EECA seeks proposals from Service Providers looking to work with Project Owners requiring heat above 85oC to deliver New Zealand-based VHTHP Projects. Supported VHTHP (including steam heat pump) projects must supply heat demands requiring a minimum of 85oC and are expected to deliver a minimum 150% increased energy efficiency (COP of 1.5). The value of developed projects will be at least $250,000.
EECA has approved a budget of up to $10 million for co-investment though this RFP to support Service.
The co-funding is to share the risk of new or underutilised technologies for industrial and commercial businesses and would contribute toward both the up-front Project development costs, and the implementation costs.
We will accept Applications as they come in and process through regular moderation meetings, through to the final acceptance date or earlier if the funding is all allocated.
Providers contracted by 30 June 2026 to develop and build projects.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
← Return To Index

Roof remediation works

Tenders.Net #: 1594168
Agency: Dunedin City Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 05/05/2026 - 04:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/

NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 15/04/2026, 22/04/2026 & 29/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.
Details: RFx ID: 33690204 - Edgar Centre Roof Remediation Works (Reference #: 11658)
The Dunedin City Council (DCC) owns the Edgar Centre Building at 116 Portsmouth Drive, Dunedin. The Edgar Centre comprises three main parts, the ‘Wool Store’ building, the More FM Arena, and the entrance and function spaces. These sections were all constructed at different times between 1978 and 1996.
The DCC are seeking to partner with a suitably qualified, capable, and experienced main contractor to undertake work to address leaks and condensation build-up in all three sections of the building. Temporary repair work is required on the Wool Store roof, while permanent remediation work is required on the More FM roof and a section of roof between buildings known as the ‘Link’.
The Edgar Centre is a heavily utilised community events and recreational facility, offering indoor courts, an arena, conference/function spaces, and administration areas for the Trust and sports organisations. It is important that the supplier can work with key stakeholders and adhere to a tight schedule to ensure safe and continued delivery of the events programme.

Development of national adaptation plan

Tenders.Net #: 1603033
Agency: Global Green Growth Institute
Location: O/S
Closing: 27/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Solomon Islands -

Please refer to the link provided for further details.
Details: Solomon Islands
---
100015216 - Development of the National Adaptation Plan (NAP) for the Solomon Islands
GGGI Solomon Islands invites eligible firms/entities to submit their proposals for a Consultancy services for Development of the National Adaptation Plan (NAP) for the Solomon Islands
PROJECT BACKGROUND:
The Global Green Growth Institute (GGGI) and the Government of Solomon Islands (SIG) seek to engage a consultancy firm (hereafter referred to as "the Consultant") to support the development and endorsement of a high-quality, implementable National Adaptation Plan (NAP) with a complementary investment plan. The consultancy will ensure that adaptation is systematically mainstreamed into governance and development frameworks, strengthen the use of climate risk information, integrate gender equality, disability, and social inclusion (GEDSI), and improve access to climate finance.
Objectives of the consultancy:
1. Develop the National Adaptation Plan (NAP): Compile and synthesise CRVA outputs, climate projections, adaptation measures, and policy reviews to deliver a comprehensive NAP that integrates climate resilience into national, sectoral, and provincial policies and plans.
2. Develop an Investment Plan: Produce a structured investment plan linked to the NAP, including a pipeline of projects, financing framework, and pathways for integration into fiscal planning and resource mobilisation.
3. Strengthen Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) Systems: Establish a harmonised MEL framework and toolkit with clear indicators (including GEDSI), aligned with national systems and international reporting requirements.
4. Strengthen communications, consultations, and knowledge sharing: Review and update the communications strategy, facilitate inclusive consultations and a national validation workshop, build capacity for climate communication, and produce knowledge products to ensure broad-based participation and national ownership.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Strategic advisory on transforming public health systems

Tenders.Net #: 1603032
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 04/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: P: +1 202-458-5858
E: CorporateProcurement@worldbank.org
Details: India
---
0002021305 - Strategic Advisory for Delivering Results and Impact under PHSPP
The overarching objective of this assignment is to provide strategic and technical advisory support to the World Bank to enhance the performance and impact of the Transforming Public Health Systems for Pandemic Preparedness (PHSPP); drawing on global best practices. During the final phase of implementation; the assignment will support the Bank through strengthened implementation monitoring; just in time strategic and technical advice to refine program design and delivery; and focused program management support to accelerate results. It will also document and synthesise implementation lessons; innovations; and constraints to inform course correction and contribute to global public goods for pandemic preparedness and resilient public health systems.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
← Return To Index

Global evidence review on economic roles & contributions of forcibly displaced people

Tenders.Net #: 1603031
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 19/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: P: +1 202-458-5858
E: CorporateProcurement@worldbank.org
Details: No Countries Assigned
---
0002021833 - The Displacement Dividend: Global Evidence on the Economic Role of Forcibly Displaced People
The International Finance Corporation (IFC) - in partnership with the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) – seeks to procure the services of a qualified consulting firm, consortium, or expert team to undertake a global evidence review on the economic roles and contributions of forcibly displaced people (refugees, internally displaced persons, and returnees).
This assignment - titled The Displacement Dividend: Building the Business Case with Global Evidence on the Economic Role of Forcibly Displaced People - will be implemented over an indicative period of six (6) months. It will involve:
• Systematic mapping of prevailing myths, biases, and dominant narratives around the economic participation of displaced populations across diverse geographies (HIC, MIC, LIC, FCV contexts).
• Global review and synthesis of quantitative and qualitative evidence demonstrating their contributions in employment, entrepreneurship, value-chain integration, private-sector service delivery, and financial inclusion.
• Development of validated indicators (including fiscal contributions and financial health metrics) for countering misconceptions and informing policy and private sector engagement.
Stakeholder engagement with displaced-led organisations, private sector actors, policymakers, and development agencies to validate findings.
• Production of key deliverables: myth & bias mapping report, comprehensive evidence-based report, global stakeholder mapping, private-sector pitch book, dissemination materials, and recommendations for a potential Phase 2 innovation component involving machine learning/AI-driven evidence updates.
This study will directly support the IFC–UNHCR Joint Initiative on Private Sector Solutions in Forced Displacement, IFC Investment services (IS), IFC Advisory services (AS), and external stakeholders, in leveraging the private sector’s capacity to create sustainable economic opportunities for forcibly displaced people and host communities.
The procurement will prioritise technical quality, methodological rigor, and global coverage.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Development of database

Tenders.Net #: 1603025
Agency: Pacific Islands Forum Fisheries Agency (FFA)
Location: O/S
Closing: 19/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Solomon Islands -

E: proper.procurement@ffa.int
Details: Solomon Islands
---
SB-FFA-528804-CS-CQS - Development of the Economic Intelligence Unit (EIU) Database
PROJECT Name: FFA: Pacific Islands Regional Oceanscape Program – Second Phase for Economic Resilience
Grant No.: IDA-E2180
The Forum Fisheries Agency (FFA) has received grant financing from the World Bank toward the cost of the Pacific Islands Regional Oceanscape Program – Second Phase for Economic Resilience (PROPER) and intends to apply part of the proceeds for the following consulting services.
FFA’s mission is to strengthen national capacity and regional cooperation to manage, control, and develop offshore tuna fisheries sustainably. The Agency supports members in exercising sovereign control over fisheries within their Exclusive Economic Zones (EEZs) and engages in regional fisheries governance, including through the Western and Central Pacific Fisheries Commission (WCPFC).
FFA is governed by the Forum Fisheries Committee (FFC), composed of national officials and ministers. The Secretariat, led by the Director-General, and has four divisions: Fisheries management, Fisheries Development, Fisheries Operations, and Corporate services.
The Pacific Islands Forum Fisheries Agency (FFA) now invites eligible consulting firms (“Consultants”) to indicate their interest in providing the services. Interested Consultants should provide information demonstrating that they have the required qualifications and relevant experience to perform the services.
The best qualified firm to carry out the services will be selected in accordance with the Consultant’s Qualifications Based Selection (CQS) method set out in the Regulations, and based on the following criteria:
FFA is looking for a firm with the following minimum qualification and experience to undertake the assignment:
• At least 10 years of experience in database architecture and data warehousing
• Proven experience developing ETL systems and business intelligence platforms
• Experience implementing secure, role-based database systems
• Strong experience in dashboard development and data visualization
• Experience in public sector or regional organizations
• Experience in fisheries economics or natural resource data systems (advantage)
• Experience working in the Pacific region (advantage)Further information can be obtained at the address below during office hours 08:00am – 16:00pm week days.

Details: Sri Lanka
---
Loan No. and Title: 4548-SRI: Power System Strengthening and Renewable Energy Integration Project (PSSREIP)
Bid No. and Title: IFB No: CEB/AGM(P)/TDE/IFB/2025/02/HLPC Power System Strengthening and Renewable Energy Integration
Project: Package 6 - Development of Renewable Energy Centre at National System control Centre (NSCC)
The National System Operator Private Limited (“the Employer”), invites sealed bids from eligible Bidders for the Development of Renewable Energy Centre at National System control Centre (NSCC)
Scope of the Project:
The project includes the design, supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of the SCADA/EMS at the National System control Centre (NSCC), National System Operator Private Limited, including integration of the Renewable Energy management System (REMS) with existing grid infrastructure. The scope further covers software configuration, operator training, documentation, and maintenance support to ensure secure and reliable system operation.
Completion period is 545 days (from the Contract Effective Date).

External IT service

Tenders.Net #: 1602900
Agency: ILO
Location: O/S
Closing: 30/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: For further details, please refer to the link provided.
Details: Jordan
---
rfx_6128_ROAS / RFQ/95/2026/DHM/ROAS - LTA for the Provision of an External IT Service Provider
The International Labour Organization (ILO) seeks to engage a qualified external IT Service Provider to support the delivery of reliable, secure, and efficient information technology services at its Jordan Country Office in Amman. This assignment aims to ensure the continuity of IT operations, strengthen user support services, maintain compliance with organizational IT policies, and enhance coordination with regional and headquarters IT structures. The selected service provider will play a critical role in supporting day-to-day IT functions, facilitating communication between end users and central IT services, and contributing to the effective management of IT infrastructure, systems, and services. Subject to the availability of funds. The engagement shall be established through a Long-Term Agreement (LTA) for an initial period of two (2) years, with the possibility of extension for an additional one (1) year, subject to satisfactory performance and the continued availability of funds.

Leadership workforce transition training program

Tenders.Net #: 1602890
Agency: Asian Development Bank (ADB)
Location: O/S
Closing: 07/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Fiji -

Allison Woodruff
E: awoodruff@adb.org
Details: Fiji
---
TA-10598 FIJ: Healthy Oceans and water security Improvement Project - 07 WAF Leadership Workforce Transition Training Program (57294-001)
The water Authority of Fiji (WAF) is a national utility responsible for the delivery of essential water and wastewater services. WAF operates in a complex and high risk environment characterised by climate pressures, infrastructure investment demands, regulatory scrutiny, and significant stakeholder and community expectations.
Objectives of the Assignment
The overall objective of the assignment is to design and deliver a structured, multi year leadership development program that strengthens governance, leadership capability, and organisational performance across WAF.
Specific objectives include:
(i) Strengthening corporate governance capability and Board–management alignment
(ii) Building consistent leadership capability across executive, management, and supervisory levels
(iii) Enhancing change leadership, risk management, and crisis leadership capacity
(iv) Improving stakeholder and community engagement capability
(v) Supporting the transition to a high performing, accountable, and service oriented organisational culture
(vi) Establishing a sustainable leadership pipeline for future leaders within WAF.

Financial modelling on supporting preparation of infrastructure projects

Tenders.Net #: 1602887
Agency: Asian Development Bank (ADB)
Location: O/S
Closing: 07/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Contact Person for Inquiries
Kiran Fatima
E: kfatima@adb.org

Project Officer
Ichiro Aoki
E: iaoki@adb.org
Details: Pakistan
---
TA-9292 REG: Strengthening Project Preparation Capacity in Asia and the Pacific - Supporting Preparation of Infrastructure Projects with Private Sector Participation in Asia Pacific (Subproject 4) - #2 PAK: Financial Modelling Firm (M6 Hyderabad-Sukkur Motorway (Sections I and II) (49407-005)
Pakistan’s road transport network carries the majority of the country’s freight and passenger traffic and remains strategically important to national logistics, trade, and economic activity. While major investments have been made to develop the north–south motorway system, a critical gap remains between Hyderabad and Sukkur, limiting the full operationalisation of the Peshawar–Karachi Motorway (PKM) corridor and increasing logistics costs, travel time, and road safety risks.
The M6 Hyderabad–Sukkur Motorway is a strategically significant national infrastructure project designed to close this missing link of the PKM corridor. The project comprises approximately 306 km of controlled-access motorway in Sindh Province, connecting the existing M-9 Motorway near Hyderabad with the M-5 Motorway at Sukkur.
Given fiscal constraints and varying commercial viability along the corridor, the National Highway Authority (NHA) is pursuing a section-wise hybrid implementation strategy. Sections I and II (approximately 121 km covering the Hyderabad - Tando Adam - Nawabshah section), expected to exhibit relatively stronger traffic demand and revenue potential, are proposed for implementation under a Public Private Partnership (PPP) modality (“the Project”), while adjoining sections may be delivered through NHA’s sovereign-financed procurement.
Under the PPP modality the private partner will be responsible for design, construction, financing, operations and maintenance of the PPP sections, subject to performance-based specifications and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). NHA will retain contract management and regulatory oversight responsibilities, including ensuring timely availability of land, right-of-way and coordination of corridor interfaces.
Feasibility study, technical investigations and environmental assessment have been undertaken by NHA (through independent consultants) previously for M6 Motorway. However, considering the time elapsed since their preparation, evolving traffic demand patterns, updated design requirements and revised cost structures, it is necessary to comprehensively revalidate, update and supplement these existing studies for sections I and II of the M6 Motorway forming part of this Project to be implemented under PPP modality.
The Asian Development Bank (ADB), through its Office of Markets Development and Public–Private Partnerships (OMDP), is supporting the Government of Pakistan and NHA for the Project, as transaction advisor across the PPP development cycle.
ADB as the transaction advisor, seeks the services of a financial advisory firm/consortium of firms (the “Consultant”) with proven experience in financial feasibility analysis, due diligence, financial modelling and transaction structuring, financial/commercial support during tendering and PPP transaction structuring and risk analysis until financial close.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*

Details: Mongolia
---
LOAN-4307 MON: Aimags and Soums Green Regional Development Investment Program - Tranche 1 - FS23 Feasibility study for Tranche 2 and Tranche 3 (49430-006)
The main objective of the consulting services is to develop a comprehensive feasibility study for subsequent tranches (Tranche 2 and Tranche 3) of the investment program including technical, financial, environment, land acquisition and resettlement, and social feasibility supported by all discipline studies, surveys, census, impact assessments, financial and economic analysis, feasibility calculations, results of consultation and campaigns, solutions on land acquisition and resettlement, draft resettlement plans, land use plan, architectural and engineering sketch drawings and layouts and registry of lessons learned, documents, information and database, draft land acquisition and resettlement plans (LARPs), Initial Environmental Examination (IEE), Environmental management Plan (EMP) for subsequent phases of the project; Environmental Assessment and Review Framework (EARF); Environmental and Social management System (ESMS); and other documents and reports required by ADB and the Government of Mongolia (GOM).
The consulting firm shall carry out the tasks as arranged with the following two activities:
(i) Activity 1: Feasibility Studies for Tranche 2 and Tranche 3;
(ii) Activity 2: Support for the preparation of the Periodic Financing Request document package for Tranche 2 and Tranche 3.

Support official recognition of Bumbat Red goat as certified strain

Tenders.Net #: 1602882
Agency: UNDP
Location: O/S
Closing: 04/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: bids.mn@undp.org
Details: Mongolia
---
UNDP-MNG-00641 / RFP/2026/010 - Support the official recognition of the Bumbat
Red Goat of Khovd aimag as a certified strain.
Improving Adaptive Capacity and Risk management of Rural Communities in Mongolia (ADAPT)’’ Project
Duration: 18 months
Scope of work: The objective of the assignment is to formally verify, in accordance with applicable laws and regulations, that the Bumbat Red Goat is adapted to high‑mountain pasture ecosystems and exhibits superior cashmere yield and quality compared to other goat breeds and strains, and to develop a policy and strategic framework, grounded in modern genetic science, to guide its sustainable breeding and improvement, and to establish enabling conditions for its effective implementation. The strategy will aim to preserve the herd’s key economic, production and biological traits, including climate resilience, capacity for weight gain under pasture grazing conditions, and meat characteristics, while maintaining cashmere quality, increasing yield at appropriate levels, and strengthening the genetic pool.

Training program for youth & adult promoters in education, comprehensive health, and life skills

Tenders.Net #: 1602870
Agency: UNFPA
Location: O/S
Closing: 04/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: The original advertisement is in Spanish and may not be translated correctly. Please refer link provided for further details.

E: argentina.office@unfpa.org
Details: Argentina
---
UNFPA-ARG-00002 - Service for implementing the process of calling for and training promoters
The UNFPA is requesting services to implement a training program for youth and adult promoters in education, comprehensive health, and life skills in the province of Misiones. The organization is expected to develop two simultaneous training programs: one for adolescents and young adults aged 14 to 24, and another for adults aged 25 and over (community leaders, adult role models, teachers, and other relevant professionals). The training cycles should include theoretical modules on relevant topics (sexual and reproductive health, mental health, violence prevention, including violence in digital environments and child sexual abuse, adolescent and youth rights, life skills, and comprehensive sexuality education) combined with training in soft skills such as teamwork, public speaking, and leadership, as well as training in tools for knowledge sharing and workshop facilitation. Coordination with local services in the province of Misiones is also expected to promote access for adolescents and young adults and foster intersectoral collaboration.
This Request for Quotation is open to all legally constituted entities that can provide the requested services and have the legal capacity to provide services in the country, or through an authorized representative.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
