Final evaluation of project

Tenders.Net #: 1602853
Agency: UN-Women
Location: O/S
Closing: 05/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: The original advertisement is in French and may not be translated correctly. Please refer link provided for further details.

E: onufemmes.achats@unwomen.org
Details: Morocco
---
UNW-MAR-2026-00011 - Completion of the final evaluation of the "Ajyal Equality" project
The “Ajyal Equality” program is being implemented jointly in Morocco, Tunisia, and Libya, as well as through a regional component. It is funded by the French Development Agency (AFD) and the French Embassy, ​​and is structured around three complementary components:
• Component 1: Support to national institutions for strengthening policy and institutional frameworks to promote gender equality, implemented by UN Women;
• Component 2: Support to civil society organizations (CSOs) for the promotion of women’s rights and gender equality, implemented by Expertise France;
• Component 3: Support to the media for gender-sensitive reporting and the promotion of egalitarian role models, also implemented by Expertise France.
The evaluation will cover all three components of the program, including their interactions and their This collective contribution to global objectives will allow for an assessment of the coherence, complementarity, and added value of the multi-stakeholder partnership between UN Women and Expertise France, the effects and changes produced through this partnership, as well as the relevance and effectiveness of the interventions carried out in the three countries and at the regional level.
The “Ajyal Equality” program is a regional program aimed at strengthening the culture of gender equality in Morocco, Tunisia, and Libya by supporting national and local actors (national institutions, civil society, media) committed to these issues. To achieve this ambitious objective, the program relies on three key areas of intervention:
• Supporting the implementation of national public policies to reduce gender inequalities (component 1)
• Supporting civil society actors in their actions to promote gender equality at the local, national, and regional levels (component 2)
• Contributing to the promotion of a culture of gender equality by Media (Component 3)

Tenders.Net #: 1602843
Agency: UNDP
Location: O/S
Closing: 07/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: procurement.ng@undp.org

Site Inspection/Pre-Bid Meeting. Will be conducted
Date: Wednesday 28th of April 2026.

Venue: United Nations House Plot 617/618 Diplomatic Drive, Central Business District, Abuja, Nigeria

Time and time zone: 11:00 am, (Nigerian Local Time)

The Prebid meeting/Site Inspection: Mandatory.
Details: Nigeria
---
UNDP-NGA-01396 - Long Term Agreement - Provision of Unarmed Private security services
UNDP Nigeria Country Office is seeking to contract the services of a reputable and experienced security service provider to Provide Unarmed Private security Service (UPSS) at UN House Abuja on behalf of UN Agencies, Funds and Programmes in Nigeria including but not limited to UNDP, UNDSS, UNICEF, UNFPA, FAO, UN Women, UNAIDS, UNESCO, WMO, UNIDO, UNIC, WHO, UNODC, ILO, UN RCO, UNOPS, UN GCO, UNIC, WFP, UNHCR, UNCHA, UNHABITAT & WIPO. The selected service provider may also be required to provide similar scope of services at and other UN offices in Nigeria.
The chosen company, hereinafter referred to as the UPSS provider for purposes of this SOW, will be required to recruit, train and manage a guard force consisting of unarmed supervisory and subordinate security personnel at the UNSMS (UN security management System) organization’s facilities in Nigeria.

Technical building & facilities maintenance

Tenders.Net #: 1602839
Agency: UNDP
Location: O/S
Closing: 08/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: procurement.ng@undp.org

Site Inspection/Pre-Bid Meeting. Will be conducted
Date: Tuesday 28th of April 2026.

Venue: United Nations House, Plot 617/618 Diplomatic Drive, Central Business District, Garki, Abuja, Nigeria

Time and time zone: 11:00 am prompt (Nigerian Local Time)

The Prebid meeting/Site Inspection: Mandatory.
Details: Nigeria
---
UNDP-NGA-01400 - Long Term Agreement - Technical Building & facilities maintenance
UNDP Nigeria Country Office is seeking to contract a highly competent, skilled and experienced professional Technical Building & facilities maintenance services company to provide Technical Building & facilities maintenance at UN House Abuja which accommodates a host of United Nations Agencies, Funds and Programmes including but not limited to UNDP, UNDSS, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA, FAO, UN Women, UNIDO, UNODC, UN RCO, UNAIDS, WMO, WIPO, UNHABITAT, UNIC, UNOPS, UNSE4ALL, UN GCO, ILO and UNESCO. This scope project description also extends to other UN Offices within Abuja and other states of the Federation for which additional personnel may be required.
The required services shall consist of routine high-level Technical Building & facilities maintenance services of all the buildings and Offices within UN House premises on daily basis during the contract period. The company shall follow global best practice methods/techniques, and use environmentally friendly consumables & products, which are designed to reduce the impact on the environment.

South Pacific Oil Ltd through its Capital Project invites sealed quotations from qualified bidders for Improvement works for SPOL Ngossi Residence.

IOC/OST/RFP/034/26 - IOC data architecture implementation plan and FAIR Ocean data charter
A. IOC Data Architecture Implementation Plan - Develop an implementation plan, which assesses existing IOC systems (IODE and GOOS) and provides the design scope, structure and governance for the future system, to meet the needs of the IOC medium term strategy (2022-2029) and Member States.
B. FAIR Ocean Data Charter – Provide a clearly articulated definition for IOC National Oceanographic Data Centres and other providers of data for FAIR data approaches, building on the IOC Data Policy and Terms of Use (2023, IOC/INF-1548).

Ministry of Finance & Economic Development
P.O. Box 67
Bairiki, Tarawa
Kiribati
P: +686 74021806
E: procurement@mfep.gov.ki

To obtain further information and inspect the Bidding Document, Bidders should contact Procurement Technical Assistance via link provided.

Procurement Technical Assistance:
Tom Williams
E: TomW@frequency.nz

A Pre-Bid meeting shall take place as follows:
Date: 30 April 2026
Location: Remote (MS Teams link shall be provided)
Time: 10:00AM (Tarawa, Kiribati time)

A site visit conducted by the Employer shall be organised.
Details: Kiribati
---
Procurement of Kiribati South Tarawa Renewable Energy Project (Phase-2), Package STREP2-P-01-rebid
===
Grant No. and Title: 1010/1011- KIR: South Tarawa Renewable Energy Project (Phase-2)
Contracts No. and Title: STREP2-P-01-rebid
Contract No. 1: Kiribati South Tarawa Renewable Energy Project (Phase-2), (STREP-2-01-rebid) a design-build contract of the floating PV plant and BESS project and other items based on the requirements of the Employer.
Contract No. 2: O&M services for STREP2-P-01-rebid - a bespoke contract defining the parties’ obligations for the operation and maintenance of the facilities under contract Nº1 for a period of twelve (12) months following their operational acceptance.
The Public Utilities Board, (PUB of Kiribati), (“the Employer”) invites sealed bids from eligible Bidders for design, supply, installation, testing & commissioning of floating PV plant and BESS including 12-month O&M for installed facilities; and supply of electric vehicles and installation of charging stations based on the requirements of the Employer (“the Works”).
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
← Return To Index

Water intensity reduction

Tenders.Net #: 1600758
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 23/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 16/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

P: +1 202-458-5858
E: CorporateProcurement@worldbank.org
Details: Egypt
---
0002021654 - water Intensity Reduction Sugar Beet Egypt
On the farming side; production occurs in a desert context (high evaporative demand; wind; and variable soil constraints including sandy/calcareous soils and salinity risk). Sugar beet’s typical total water requirement is on the order of 550 - 750 mm per growing period (climate dependent). It is particularly sensitive to water deficits during emergence and roughly the first month after emergence; frequent light irrigations are therefore often preferred during establishment; including to manage soil crusting and top soil salinity. After establishment; moderate deficit irrigation strategies may be feasible in some contexts if carefully timed to protect sugar yield and recovery. The Client has already invested substantially in centre pivot irrigation systems for existing beet fields and is not willing to replace this installed pivot equipment. The assignment will therefore prioritise optimisation of existing pivot systems and operational practices (management; monitoring; maintenance; controls); and will only consider alternative irrigation technologies for future expansion areas where new equipment decisions are still open.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
← Return To Index

Hydropower development projects (various works)

Tenders.Net #: 1600575
Agency: Asian Development Bank (ADB)
Location: O/S
Closing: 01/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 23/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

Pushkar Manandhar
E: pmanandhar@adb.org
Details: India
---
TA-10755 IND: Supporting the Project Preparation of Nagaland Hydropower Development Projects - Consulting firm (57203-002)
The technical assistance (TA) 10755-IND will support the state government of Nagaland in India, specifically the Department of Power, Nagaland (DPN), in the project preparation and due diligence of two ensuing hydropower subprojects: the 24-megawatt (MW) Zungki hydropower subproject and the 42-MW Lower Tizu hydropower subproject. This support will enable the sustainable development of hydropower in Nagaland, ensuring that technical, environmental, gender mainstreaming, and social safeguards are fully integrated into project design and execution. In doing so, it will promote inclusive growth, enhance the state’s energy security, and ensure that hydropower development is carefully balanced with the need to maintain functioning ecosystems, especially Nagaland's rivers and forests that have high biodiversity value for their endemism and that support Indigenous People’s livelihoods.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Services for skills mobility program

Tenders.Net #: 1597347
Agency: IOM
Location: O/S
Closing: 04/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 01/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity link & details updated.

E: iomegbids@iom.int
Details: Egypt
---
30000022954 (30000022954-3) - RFQ for the Provision of services for a Skills Mobility Program
To facilitate the creation of a mutually beneficial talent development and mobility partnership between Egypt and Austria.
The EAGLE project aims at facilitating skills development and mobility between the Egypt and Austria.
The project design is axed around investing in the further education of qualified workers in Egypt in the transportation sector, as well as providing initial work experience in professions in which skilled workers are needed in Austria. Eagle will develop a curriculum focusing on technical vocational training (TVET) in the field of metal technology and processing for the transport sector, in particular on modern welding techniques.
Subsequently, a certain number of skilled workers will move to Austria in the framework of the Red- White-Red Card (RWR Card) scheme.
In addition to imparting specific specialist knowledge, EAGLE attaches particular importance to the promotion of linguistic skills, German and English, basic as well as understanding of European values, principles and traditions. The aim is to accelerate and facilitate integration in Austria and in international companies in Egypt along with facilitating adaptation to the living, working and business environment.
A total of 135 people will be trained as part of the project. The prerequisite for participation is a completed apprenticeship in metal technology and processing that is comparable to Austrian standards. The training package consists of a six-months training course in technical and soft skills. Furthermore, three-month internships are arranged to increase the employability of the EAGLE participants. For 100 participants, who wish to emigrate from Egypt, German courses and an A2 certificate are offered (the so-called "Away-Track") for a total of 10-months. The 35 participants, who are placed on the Egyptian labor market will receive English courses (the so-called "Home-Track") for a total of three-months. All participants receive an EAGLE scholarship for the duration of the six-month training.

PMCSC for construction of highway (various services)

Tenders.Net #: 1596336
Agency: Asian Development Bank (ADB)
Location: O/S
Closing: 23/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 18/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

Project Officer
Kamshat Sakhiyeva
KazAvtoZhol "National Company" Joint Stock Company
E: kamshat.sakhiyeva@qaj.kz
Details: Kazakhstan
---
LOAN-4631 KAZ: Central Asia Regional Economic Cooperation Corridors 3 and 6 Turkistan Oblast Highway (Saryagash Bypass) Project - SBRP/PMCSC/C01/02 Selection of Consulting services for: Project management and Construction Supervision Consultant (PMCSC) for the Construction of Saryagash Bypass Highway (0–102.6 km) (54217-001)
The primary objective of this Consultant assignment is to ensure the successful delivery of the Saryagash Bypass Road Project in line with its stated goals, timeline, and quality requirements.
The Consultant will have a dual role as (a) Project management Consultant for overall project management and implementation; and (b) Construction Supervision Consultant/FIDIC Engineer for the supervision of the Design-Build contract(s) for Saryagash Bypass. The specific objectives of the Consultant assignment are:
• Support Project Implementation: Provide end-to-end project management support throughout the project lifecycle - from procurement and contract award, through design and construction, and into the maintenance period - to ensure the project stays on schedule, within budget, and meets all technical specifications and performance standards.
• Perform the duties of the Construction Supervision Consultant/FIDIC Engineer: Undertake all functions of “the Engineer” as defined in the FIDIC Yellow Book contracts. This includes reviewing and approving the contractors’ designs, issuing necessary instructions or determinations, certifying work progress and payments, administering contract terms, evaluating and responding to claims, and ensuring the works are carried out in full compliance with the contract requirements.
The Consultant/Engineer shall make engineering decisions in consultation with the Client as needed.
• Ensure Compliance with ADB Policies and National regulations: Monitor and ensure compliance with all ADB requirements and loan covenants at the project level, particularly in the areas of procurement, environmental and social safeguards, gender actions, financial management, and reporting, as well as with applicable National regulations. The Consultant will help the Client meet its obligations under ADB’s Safeguard Policy Statement (2009) and other applicable policies by instituting proper monitoring systems and reporting mechanisms.
• Coordinate Stakeholders: Serve as a central coordination point between the Client/PMU, the contractors for each lot, ADB, and other stakeholders. The Consultant will ensure smooth communication and information flow, align efforts of different parties, and promptly address any interface issues between civil works contracts, utility agencies, local authorities, and the community.
• Capacity Building: Transfer knowledge and build capacity within the Client’s/PMU’s team. Through on-the-job training, workshops, and joint working, the Consultant should enhance the PMU’s capabilities in up-to-date project management practices, procurement, contract administration, quality assurance, and maintenance planning, thereby contributing to institutional strengthening (one of the project’s objectives).
• Achieve Project Development Outcomes: Ultimately, help align the implementation process with the project’s development objectives (improved regional connectivity, reduced travel times, enhanced road safety, etc.). The Consultant’s performance will be evaluated by how effectively the project delivers its intended outputs and outcomes as per the Design and Monitoring Framework (DMF) - for example, timely completion of 102 km of new highway meeting all standards, successful operation of a tolling system, improved traffic conditions in Saryagash, and achievement of target indicators for road safety and travel efficiency.
• Implementation of SEAH. Implement actions and targets related Sexual Exploitation, Abuse, and Harassment (SEAH).” Provide resources and implement all activities and achieve targets to the prevention and management of incidents of SEAH as set out in the Gender Assessment and Action Plan (GAAP), including the development and implementation of the Code of Conduct for contractors and workers and establishment and operations of the Grievance Mechanism to address SEAH complaints.

PPPC PDMF
PPP Center of the Philippines (attached to NEDA)
E: PDMF@ppp.gov.ph
Details: Philippines
---
LOAN-4577 PHI: Project Development and Monitoring facility - PDMF/CS-014 Project Preparation and Transaction Advisory services for the Operation and maintenance of the Panguil Bay Bridge Project (56369-001)
The main objectives of the project preparation and transaction advisory services include the following:
a) Conduct a business case to determine the preliminary economic and financial viability of the Project through the following schemes: PPP under RA 11966 or (“PPP Code”) and its IRR, Official ODA, General Appropriations Act (GAA), or any combination thereof.
b) Conduct a full feasibility study if the Project is to be pursued through PPP.
c) Prepare bid/tender documents, provide advisory and transaction support to the DPWH, and ensure effective bid process and contract execution until the financial close of the Project if the Project is to be pursued through PPP.

Common Issues + FAQs

What does 'Availability Unknown' Mean?

Tenders.Net does not know of any electronic documentation or electronic tender boxes for this opportunity at this time. Please refer the tender contact details instead.

If any such information is later released, we will send out the advertisement again with the [Updated] flag.


I Get 'Page Not Found' when I click on a link.

Please make sure you have an active connection to the internet and have not used up your data limit. If your connection is fine then the linked site may be temporarily down. We mark any link that goes outside of Tenders.Net with an asterisk ( * ).

If the link continues to fail you should then use the tender contact details instead for assistance.


I can't log in to the 'Documentation' or 'Lodgement' links.

If you are being taken to an external site - the link will be marked with an asterisk ( * ) - then your Tenders.Net login details will not work. You will need another access to that site.

If you are asked to connect to www.tenders.net and do not have an access please contact us or register to get a login. If you cannot remember your access you can retrieve your password here.


What is a 'Selective' or 'Invitation Only' Tender?

Unlike public tendering where anyone can make a response, some opportunities are restricted only to those who have been directly invited. These are sometimes the result of a public EOI.

You will not normally be able to respond to these opportunities unless you have been invited.

Empower your organisations procurement practices with Tenders.Net. By advertising your RFx opportunity on Tenders.Net you reach thousands of suppliers (like you!) through our dedicated notification system.

Designed to meet the requirements of Government, the system provides audit logs for your entire opportunity, dedicated cloud lodgement facilities, supplier forums and much more.
